raw_text:
Mixed Experience with Saudi Airlines I travelled to Saudi Arabia with Saudi Airlines for Omra this year. My outbound trip from Cairo was a pleasant experience. The crew was professional, the flight was stable from takeoff to landing, and the overall service met my expectations. Unfortunately, the return trip was the exact opposite.

Return Trip Issues:
- The experience began poorly during boarding. A visibly agitated Saudi Airlines employee displayed an aggressive attitude toward passengers with Egyptian passports, which was unprofessional and unwelcoming.
- The plane on the return trip vastly differed from the one on the outbound one. It seemed older, with excessively worn-out, uncomfortable, uncleaned washrooms and walls around and poorly maintained seats.
- The cabin crew was disappointing. They wore uniforms different from the usual Saudi Airlines crew and appeared untrained or inexperienced, lacking the professionalism seen on the outbound flight. Their performance felt more like that of students in training than of seasoned professionals.
- The flight itself was nerve-wracking. After crossing the Red Sea, the plane began shaking and swinging, creating a sense of instability that lasted until we landed. The turbulence was persistent and frightening, making me worry for my safety.
The landing was equally unsettling. It felt unstable and rough, and I thought we were nearly crashing.

While my outbound experience with Saudi Airlines was commendable, the return trip raised serious concerns about the consistency of service, aircraft maintenance, and crew training. I hope the airline addresses these issues to ensure a safer and more reliable experience for passengers in the future.

raw_text:
Horrible Experience with Saudi Airlines – Worst Airline Ever! I had an awful experience on Saudi Airlines Flight SV 120 from London to Dhaka on 29th November 2024. They completely destroyed one of my suitcases, making it entirely unusable—part of the luggage was literally missing! To make matters worse, they lost another piece of luggage weighing 23kg. Shockingly, I wasn’t the only victim—four other families on the same flight also had missing luggage.

After a grueling 13-hour flight, imagine standing in front of the luggage belt for two hours, exhausted, with tired eyes, a sick wife, and a 2-year-old baby, waiting for bags that never arrived. Not a single apology from the airline staff—absolutely no empathy or accountability.

It took 48 hours for them to return the missing luggage, but only after I called them over 25 times! By the time it arrived, everything inside—clothes and food—was ruined due to mishandling. The entire ordeal left me frustrated and drained.

The service onboard was equally appalling. There was no menu choice—just one option: rice and peas! I don’t mind vegetarian food occasionally, but being forced to eat that with no alternative felt like I had been cheated. I paid good money for this flight and expected decent service, but what I got was a complete shambles.

Saudi Airlines, you’ve truly earned the title of the worst airline in the world. Never again!!!!🤬🤬🤬

raw_text:
A Disappointing Experience As a Saudi, I take great pride in our national institutions, but my recent experience with Saudi Airlines was nothing short of a disaster. I am compelled to share this to warn others and, hopefully, prompt much-needed improvement.

From start to finish, the entire process was riddled with mismanagement and misinformation. Over the course of several phone calls, I was given contradictory information by their employees, each time promising a resolution or providing instructions that ultimately led to wasted time and money. I trusted their guidance, only to find myself in worse situations with every interaction. This negligence cost me thousands of dollars.

The final straw was arriving at the airport as instructed, only to find their office closed. Despite assurances over the phone that I should come in person, there was no one available to assist. To make matters worse, when I sought help, the staff at the airport were unhelpful, dismissive, and even went so far as to accuse me of lying about the situation. Adding insult to injury, the supervisor I was told would assist never showed up, leaving me stranded and without any recourse.

This experience was not just disappointing; it was deeply frustrating and embarrassing. As a Saudi, I find it unacceptable that our national carrier operates with such poor standards of service and accountability. This is not the image we should project to the world, nor is it the level of service customers deserve.

I will never fly with Saudi Airlines again and strongly urge others to think twice before choosing them. For an airline that carries the pride of a nation, this experience was a shameful failure. Major reforms and improvements are desperately needed to restore any faith in their services.

raw_text:
First time flyer with Saudi Airline As a first time traveler on Saudi Airlines i was slightly nervous after reading mix reviews. But i am glad it turned out very well. Everything from boarding the plane to landing ran smoothly. Aircraft was clean with no funny odour. You could tell that hygiene is one of top priorities. Above average leg space in economy. Food serving was immaculate with fresh hot food options between veggie and non-veg including snacks with tea/coffee. Like the fact that this airline still keeping the tradition of welcoming kids with toys etc. Not many airlines still serve the stuff that helps long journey but this one does. Option of requesting an allergy free food online. In flight entertainment available. Landing was smooth.
Suggestions:
- Make the website more user friendly with strong communication.
- Cabin crew up skill to communicate in English.

There are certainly better options available for travellers.

raw_text:
One of the worst experience I had in 28 years of flying. I experienced very badly with this airline. We got a connecting flight from Istanbul to Saudi Arabia, then back to Thailand. The flight was around 18.45, however, the flight was delayed to 22.05, which caused us to unable to go with our connecting flight (which is 1.50am). This matter was not informed, nor the airline feel responsible. We were told the airport in Saudi Arabia would offer us options. The attitude was 'ZERO CARE'from the counter service, and even chased us away!

The worst part was when we arrived at Jeddah airport, we have to wait from 1.50 am till almost 5 am for the matter. The attitude of the employee on the connecting flight counter was rude as well.Naturally, the airline offered us free accommodation and sad meals, HOWEVER, we could not gain access to our luggage, nor given key cards. The air condition inside the room was too cold, nor could we can adjust the temperature. By cannot gain access to the key card, given sad meals that always arrived late and had to wait until feeling starving, living inside the cold room without gaining access to the luggage, I felt like being treated as a prisoner.

We were struck for 3 days, 2 nights. At first, the hotel not even plan to give us meals on the third day, in which our flight was around 1 am, until we talked to the manager.

Never fly with this airline again. It's a shame the airplane was nice, but that's it. It's understandable about the delay due to the New Year period, but at least be more humanized and be more responsible to us as we are not the ones who caused a delay in the first place!

AVOID THIS AIRLINES AT ALL COST! Check the story below Please avoid this airlines at all cost!
My first experience flying with SV was horrible. They broken my luggage but didn’t even respond to my claim.
Then I was stupidly fallen into their 2nd trap due to a good Christmas deal.
This time was even worse! I wasn’t being informed that I need a transit visa if my flight has two transits within Saudi Arabia and it wasn’t free, and must be applied 72h before my flight. Luckily my UK BRP card saved me!

Their bureaucracy was the worst I’ve ever seen! I took them almost an hour to just filling a simply file and a stamp. I had to approached many immigration desk and told them that my connection flight is due soon. FYI, there were a lot of immigration officers but only few of them working, most of them were just chitchatting instead of trying to reduce the queue.

Landed in Jakarta, then the epic ultimate disaster happened! I landed without my luggages. They missed my luggages !!!
I was really mad because I had to fly to another country after 3 days, and they were the most unprofessional human being I’ve ever encountered. I had to repeat and went down to a very basic English to make them understand my claim.

I had to wear the same clothes for 2 days and on the 3rd day I decided to buy new clothes for my next trip. Then the luggages arrived few hours before my another flight.

Surprise! They informed me that they couldn’t compensate the delayed luggages due to security reason (which I asked them to explain further but then they never came back)

PLEASE DO NOT FLY WITH THIS AIRLINES!

Now i look at the flight we booked and it is still open for booking, but now 300 euros more per ticket!! Same flight number (SV 216) and departure times!! raw_text: Lovely flight! I wanted to write a review after seeing all the real negative comments which as a nervous flyer scared the life out of me. I was so petrified but the second I got on the plane my anxiety instantly disappeared. The music they play while getting on was so relaxing and calming my nerves was gone. The flight attendants were so lovely and accommodating. You get given blankets, pillows and a little travel kit with eye masks, socks, toothbrush and toothpaste, ear plugs and head phones. They had lots of programs to chose from but not a wide range of choice but I was happy with it. You get given a little pizza, and some sort of curry dish. This was okay but as someone who is a really fussy eater I struggled to eat any of it. But for breakfast time they had muffins, crossaints, OJ, Apple juice and pineapple juice with bottles of water. I was very happy with the overall travel but with all comments about lost luggage I decided to stick with hand luggage so I could keep it on my person! But please if your nervous don’t be it was very pleasant!!

raw_text:
Évitez des vols avec escale à Riyadh et Djeddah Notre voyage était de Phuket à Genève avec une escale à Riyadh et une à Djeddah . Nulle part sur tous les mails que j'ai reçu il etait mentionné que nous devrions avoir un visa pour le vol en transit entre Riyadh et Djeddah. Arrivé à Riyadh tous les passagers qui suivent le même vol on été obligé de faire un Visa pour un vol Nacional. Ce que représente 100 CHF par personne. Dans mon cas 4 personnes, 400 CHF.
C'est dommage parce que les avions et l'équipage étaient très bien.
Alors faites attention si vous devez faire un vol interne vous serez obligé de faire le visa à 100 CHF par passager.
translated_text: Avoid flights with layovers in Riyadh and Jeddah. Our trip was from Phuket to Geneva with a stop in Riyadh and one in Jeddah. Nowhere in all the emails I received was it mentioned that we would need a visa for the transit flight between Riyadh and Jeddah. Upon arrival in Riyadh, all passengers on the same flight were forced to obtain a visa for a domestic flight, which costs 100 CHF per person. In my case, for 4 people, that’s 400 CHF. It’s a shame because the planes and crew were very good. So be careful if you have to take a domestic flight; you will be required to get a visa at 100 CHF per passenger.

raw_text:
Non mi aspettavo fosse così bella Mi sono trovata benissimo con questa compagnia. Il volo l'abbiamo pagato poco, il personale è estremamente cordiale. Non ho trovato niente di meno rispetto a compagnie che costano il triplo. Cuscini, coperte, schermi, selezioni di film. Ci portavano sempre cibo, snack, bevande. Ogni ora quasi una merendina.
Chiaramente tasto dolente sul cibo essendo italiana ed essendo questa una compagnia dell'arabia saudita. Però tutto top.
translated_text: I didn't expect it to be so nice. I had a great experience with this airline. We paid little for the flight, and the staff is extremely friendly. I found nothing less compared to airlines that cost three times as much. Pillows, blankets, screens, movie selections. They always brought us food, snacks, drinks. Almost every hour, there was a treat. Clearly, a sore point regarding the food, being Italian and this being a Saudi Arabian airline. But everything else was top-notch.

The company and the fleet are quite disappointing. NOT RECOMMENDED TO FLY WITH SAUDIA if you can do it with any other company.

raw_text:
Boa relação qualidade preço É a segunda vez que viajo com a saudia, a primeira fui para Kuala Lumpur, desta vez foi Manila ida e volta. O voo tem paragem em jedah, o aeroporto de jedah ainda tem muito que evoluir, faltam lojas, espaços de restauração, entretenimento, o nosso vôo de ligação era por volta das 3 da manhã foi alterado para as seis e depois para as oito o que provocou um grande desconforto na espera pois não tínhamos muito por onde escolher para comer nem um lugar mais confortável para descansar. Quanto ao vôo, dentro da normalidade, uma vantagem para mim é o facto de servirem as refeições e retirarem imediatamente depois os tabuleiros, não deixam as pessoas uma hora de tabuleiro na frente. Até á data tinha apanhado assistentes de bordo simpáticas, a de hoje era desagradável.
Outro ponto negativo é que quando ficamos no fim das filas já não há escolha na comida, hoje já não pudemos escolher panquecas ao pequeno almoço e na ida também esgotou o prato e os snacks
translated_text: Good value for money. This is the second time I travel with Saudia; the first time was to Kuala Lumpur, and this time it was to Manila round trip. The flight has a stop in Jeddah, which still has a lot to improve; there are few shops, dining spaces, and entertainment. Our connecting flight was around 3 AM, which was changed to six and then to eight, causing great discomfort during the wait as we had little choice for food and no comfortable place to rest. As for the flight, it was normal; one advantage for me is that they serve meals and immediately take away the trays, not leaving people with trays in front of them for an hour. Until now, I had encountered friendly flight attendants, but today's was unpleasant. Another negative point is that when we are at the end of the lines, there is no choice in food; today we could not choose pancakes for breakfast, and on the way there, the dish and snacks were also sold out.

raw_text: Agréablement surprise par la compagnie J’ai été agréablement surprise par les services proposés par Saudia Airlines. Dès l’embarquement, on reçoit une petite serviette chaude pour se rafraîchir les mains, accompagnée d’une trousse contenant des chaussettes antidérapantes, un kit avec brosse à dents et dentifrice, ainsi qu’un masque pour dormir. Même mon enfant a reçu une attention particulière avec une trousse adaptée, un chargeur à trois têtes, et des petits cahiers pour dessiner. Le personnel était aux petits soins tout au long du vol, toujours attentif et souriant. J’ai également beaucoup apprécié la petite prière du voyageur diffusée avant le décollage, qui ajoute une belle touche spirituelle à l’expérience. Le vol s’est déroulé à merveille, et je n’hésiterai pas à voyager à nouveau avec cette compagnie. Je recommande vivement !
translated_text: Pleasantly surprised by the airline. I was pleasantly surprised by the services offered by Saudia Airlines. From boarding, we received a small warm towel to refresh our hands, accompanied by a kit containing non-slip socks, a toothbrush and toothpaste kit, as well as a sleep mask. Even my child received special attention with an appropriate kit, a three-headed charger, and small notebooks to draw. The staff was attentive and smiling throughout the flight. I also appreciated the little prayer for travelers broadcasted before takeoff, which adds a nice spiritual touch to the experience. The flight went wonderfully, and I will not hesitate to travel again with this airline. I highly recommend it!

raw_text: Meglio di così... Compagnia aerea TOP. Nulla da invidiare alle altre grandi compagnie arabe. Buon livello di intrattenimento (qualcosa anche in italiano), aereo comodo anche in economy, il cibo servito è fenomenale.
translated_text: Better than this... Top airline. Nothing to envy from other major Arab airlines. Good level of entertainment (some in Italian), comfortable aircraft even in economy, the food served is phenomenal.

raw_text: Unfassbar verantwortungsloser Service, fehlende Gepäckstücke Wir sind gestern mit einer Gruppe von 60 Personen für unsere Rückreise von Jeddah nach Frankfurt 3h vorher am Flughafen in Jeddah angekommen. Für den gesamten Flug war nur EIN Check-in Schalter geöffnet. Die Verantwortliche am Schalter war extrem gelassen und langsam. Zwischendurch gab es einen Wechsel, alles im Schneckentempo. 3 Stunden lang standen unsere Freunde in der Schlange!!! Als wir am Gate ankamen war schon die Rede vom Last Call. Wir sprachen die Mitarbeiter dort an und erzählten von der Hälfte der Gruppe, die noch oben am Check in Schalter steht. Sie reagierten total unhöflich und meinten nur, dass sie von oben keine Nachricht bekommen haben und in 5min schließen. Es war ein riesen Stress. Im Endeffekt haben sie nur 30min gewartet und danach dicht gemacht, sodass 7 Personen aus unserer Gruppe, darunter eine Familie mit Kleinkind und eine Frau im Rollstuhl vor geschlossenen Türen standen und nicht mitfliegen konnten. Um sie hat sich keiner gekümmert. Sie wurden sogar beschuldigt obwohl die Schuld einzig und allein an den Arbeitern am Checkin Schalter lag. Als wir in Frankfurt ankamen erfuhren wir noch, dass die Gepäckstücke unvollständig geladen wurden, sodass wir alle eine Verlustanzeige machen mussten. Dort sagte man uns, dass sie bereits seit 3 Tagen das gleiche Problem mit der Airline haben. Unfassbar. Nie wieder.

Esperienza più che positiva. ho prenotato il mio viaggio per Dubai con scalo a Riyadh e in seguito ho letto le recensioni, terribili.. mi è venuta un’ansia addosso incredibile. Invece la mia esperienza è stata più che positiva, il personale è veramente molto cordiale ed educato, gli aerei sono enormi e c’è spazio per stare ben seduti! non capisco perché tutti questi commenti negativi, io sono rimasta veramente molto soddisfatta! Oltre al fatto che ho pagato solo per una tratta il posto a sedere e sono stata accanto al mio fidanzato tutte e 4 le tratte. Ottima compagnia!

La compañía no debería existir Le pondría 0 estrellas si pudiera.
Tenía un vuelo programado para un martes, pero lo cambiaron unilateralmente al viernes, sin opción ni aviso previo. Esto nos hace perder actividades valoradas en miles de euros, sin posibilidad de reembolso.

Lo peor es que nos avisaron del cambio el mismo día del vuelo original, cuando ya estábamos en camino a Barcelona desde otra ciudad. Inaceptable.

Dicen estar intentando solucionarlo, pero a estas alturas ya nos han arruinado gran parte del viaje. Desde luego, no volveré a viajar con esta compañía.
translated_text:
The company should not exist I would give 0 stars if I could.
I had a flight scheduled for a Tuesday, but they unilaterally changed it to Friday, without option or prior notice. This caused us to lose activities valued at thousands of euros, with no possibility of refund.
The worst is that they informed us of the change on the same day as the original flight, when we were already on the way to Barcelona from another city. Unacceptable.
They say they are trying to fix it, but by now they have ruined a large part of the trip. Certainly, I will not travel with this company again.

raw_text:
Decent no BS service. Overall, I would consider this airline as being on par with Oman Air and Turkish Airlines.
To start of with the good things, the
The meals all tasted great in both economy and business, and were a decent portion size too. Me and my family of 5 were also lucky enough to get upgraded for free to business class on one of my flights from Lahore to Jeddah, which was AMAZING and very kind of the airline. The crew on the business class were very smiley, warm and welcoming, and the seats were huge with, of course, an amazing full flat comfy bed. We were lucky enough to be able to experience the newer renovated business cabin. The IFE selection was great, with the same movie selections for economy. The leg room in economy was also good.
Now for the cons, the flight attendants in the economy cabin were very moody on all 3 of my economy flights. They had no smiles on their faces whatsoever all throughout the flight and gave a moody face whenever someone asked for a favour. I also had to ask about 50x for some headphones from the SAME crew member, who kept replying “ok” until i had to get up and ask a different crew member for some as the other one never bought me any. Also, the plane going from Jeddah to Lahore was very outdated to the point where my tray table was sloped down at an angle, causing my drink to spill on the poor flight attendant as he was putting my drink down on the table (maybe it was due to the fact it was an old B777).
Overall, if you can, fly business class if you can. However, the economy is great if you are not the type of person to care about the overall entertainment/ hospitality factor. (Just make sure you bring your own headphones in case :P).

Don't trust the good reviews on Uzbek Airway I have flown with Uzbek Airways many times, not because I like the airline but because I don't have a choice. It is the only airline that offers regular flights to Tashkent. In 7 months I have flown with them 5 times and you can trust me when I say it is by far the worst airline I have flown with. For a cheap fare, one should not expect the same kind of service we would get from a 5 star airline, but the least a passenger could expect is politeness from the cabin crew. I really feel the crew must be trained to be rude to passengers onboard.

During the flight, when I asked the crew for water, I was literally snubbed at by the crew who looked so unhappy. I actually witnessed a crew raising his voice at an elderly passenger when she wanted to use the toilet. I told the lady there was no point in complaining to the company because nobody will cares.

Surprisngly, there was once I was upgraded to the Business Class using my miles and the service was good. It just means the airline will only pay attention to you if you pay more. Such discriminatory attitude is revolting, to say the least.

Please, please don't trust those good reviews, they must either be written by the staff from Uzbek Airline to deflect the honest reviews about airline or by passengers who flew Business Class. Based on my many trips on Uzbek Airway, one thing they have done well - they have consistently be bad.

raw_text:
Disappointing Experience – Paid for Premium Seat, Got Nothing in Return I booked a Flynas flight and paid extra for a Premium (Extra Legroom) seat due to my medical condition – I was recovering from two recent surgeries and needed additional space and comfort.

To my surprise, I was placed in a standard seat (6B) with no extra legroom, no added comfort, and no explanation or compensation. When I politely asked the crew if I could move to one of the empty spacious seats to relieve my pain, I was coldly told to “just endure it.”

To make things worse, Flynas labels standard seats as “spacious” on their booking system, even though they clearly are not. This is misleading and dishonest marketing.

raw_text:
Finger weg von dieser Airline! Kundenservice gleich NULL Unser Rückflug von Bangkok nach Frankfurt via Jeddah wurde zunächst 2x verschoben, bis er am Ende ganz gestrichen wurde. Der Flug wurde dann von Saudia Airlines umgebucht auf Swiss - allerdings erst am nächsten Tag. Jetzt sind wir seit Monaten mit Saudia im Schriftverkehr, um eine Entschädigung zu bekommen. Von der Airlina aus haben wir erst vor Kurzem für 2 Personen € 188,- erhalten (!!!!) für eine fast 12-stündige Verspätung. Bzgl. der zusätzlichen Übernachtung, die wir in Bangkok machen mussten, haben wir noch keine Erstattung erhalten. Wir werden immer und immer wieder darauf hingewiesen, ein "Formblatt" auf der Internetseite auszufüllen, welches dann nicht hochgeladen werden kann. Andere Kommunikationswege werden nicht anerkannt seitens der Airline. Das Schlimmste ist, dass die genau wissen, dass man von Europa aus keinerlei rechtliche Handhabe hat und wollen das aussitzen. Ich bin jetzt dazu übergegangen, fast täglich einen Reminder zu schicken.
By the way: auch auf dem Hinflug hatten wir in Riad eine über 4-stündige Verspätung auf den Anschlussflug nach Bangkok. Dort am Flughafen haben wir dann mitten in der Nacht doch jeder eine kleine Dose Cola bekommen! Dies wurde auf einer Strichliste festgehalten, damit ja niemand eine 2. Dose holt!
FINGER WEG von dieser Airline, auch wenn sie unschlagbare Preise hat. Es lohnt sich nicht, denn der Ärger überwiegt.
translated_text: Stay away from this airline! Customer service is basically ZERO. Our return flight from Bangkok to Frankfurt via Jeddah was initially postponed twice, then finally canceled. The flight was rebooked by Saudia Airlines to Swiss - but only the next day. We have been in correspondence with Saudia for months to get compensation. From the airline, we recently received only €188 for 2 people (!!!!) for an almost 12-hour delay. We have not yet received reimbursement for the additional overnight stay we had to make in Bangkok. We are repeatedly told to fill out a "form" on the website, which cannot be uploaded. Other communication channels are not recognized by the airline. The worst part is that they know that from Europe you have no legal recourse and want to wait it out. I have now started sending reminders almost daily. By the way: on the outbound flight, we had a delay of over 4 hours in Riyadh for the connecting flight to Bangkok. At the airport, in the middle of the night, we each got a small can of cola! This was recorded on a tally sheet so that no one would get a second can! STAY AWAY from this airline, even if they have unbeatable prices. It's not worth it because the frustration outweighs.

Kaba, hadsiz ve saygısızı Çalışan!! Hayatımızın en berbat deneyimi. 6 kişi umre seyahatimizden dönerken Bağaj işlemi esnasında Hassan isimli çalışan çok kaba ve saygısızca muamelede bulundu. Netice olacak 90 kilo bağaj için ücret ödedik ve 15-25 kg arası çantalara yerleştirdik, ama yardımcı olmak yerine kaba tavırlarla bize zorluk çıkardılar.

translated_text: Rude, disrespectful employee!! The worst experience of our lives. Six of us returning from Umrah, during baggage processing, an employee named Hassan was very rude and disrespectful. Despite paying for 90kg extra baggage through Flynas and proving it, the system didn't show it, and he humiliated us in front of many people, telling us to unload our baggage. Another employee checked the system and confirmed we were right, but Hassan insisted we had to arrange all bags as 15kg each and showed no understanding, making us struggle for an hour in front of everyone. We paid for 90kg and packed bags between 15-25kg, but instead of helping, he made things difficult with rude behavior. Another employee said 1-2kg extra wouldn't be a problem, but rude, bald Hassan wanted to make an issue even over 1-2kg, and after we objected, he had to back down after a phone call with his friend! His disrespect continued. When we said why cause unnecessary trouble after Umrah, he rudely replied 'I don't care about your Umrah!' A complete disgrace and a disgraceful employee! Flynas usually accepts carry-on as regular baggage, but this employee rejected it. He caused us trouble in every way and crossed the line. Because of him, I will never fly Flynas again and will spread this disgrace. I used to always recommend Flynas! We will complain about this employee to all authorities! This rudeness will not go unpunished!

Don't fly with Saudia air I am writing this review out of sheer frustration and complete disappointment with saudi air. This airline has provided the worst travel experience I have ever encountered.

Upon landing, I discovered that my checked luggage was lost. While losing a bag is an unfortunate event that can happen, the complete and utter lack of communication and customer support from Souldia Rayves is what makes this situation truly unacceptable.

It has been three to four days since my flight, and I have had no success in contacting anyone from the airline. Their customer service phone lines are perpetually busy or go unanswered, and my emails have been completely ignored. I have no information, no tracking number, and no idea where my bag is or what is being done to find it.

To leave a passenger without their essential belongings for days and provide absolutely zero updates is unprofessional, irresponsible, and shows a blatant disregard for customer care. I am stuck without my personal items and have received no assistance whatsoever.

I strongly advise anyone considering flying with Saudia air to think twice. If anything goes wrong with your flight, you will be left completely on your own. A company that refuses to communicate with its customers does not deserve any business.

THIS AIRLINE SHOULD SHUT DOWN ITS OPERATIONS Hands down the worst airline I’ve ever flown with. Flynas lost my luggage — and then had the audacity to tell me they don’t offer refunds or compensation. No apology, no accountability, nothing. Just “file a report” and wait forever for a response that never comes.

Their customer service is beyond useless. I spent hours calling, emailing, and even messaging them on social media. I either got ignored or fed scripted responses that led nowhere. At one point, a representative told me “it’s not our responsibility” — excuse me? You lost my bag, not the other way around.

To make matters worse, the staff at the airport were rude, dismissive, and completely unhelpful. No updates, no empathy, no effort. It felt like they genuinely didn’t care about passengers at all.

If you value your time, your belongings, or your sanity — DO NOT fly with Flynas. Cheap tickets are not worth the stress, disrespect, or financial loss. I’ll never book with them again, and I’ll warn everyone I can to stay far away.

raw_text:
Madrid Bangkok en famille Nous avions un peu peur vu les avis mais les prix bien plus bas que la concurrence nous ont fait valider les vols (Madrid - Djeddah - Bangkok), soit 4 vols aller/retour.

Je précise que nous sommes une famille de 4 (2 adultes et 2 enfants) et avons l'habitude des longs courriers avec plusieurs compagnies (air caraïbes, air France, Oman air, Turkish Airlines,...).

Et bien nous n'avons rien à redire! Tout d'abord, les 4 vols étaient à l'heure (déjà un bon point). Le service à bord était très bien : petite serviette pour les mains à l'entrée, petite pochette en tissu avec cache yeux, écouteur, brosse à dents…, des repas convenables, le personnel très agréable qui passe régulièrement proposer des verres d'eau, un choix de film correct,... bref une classe éco classique.
Nous lisions ici et là que les shorts étaient interdit, c'est faux! au retour de Thaïlande de nombreuses personnes ont voyagées en short.
La seule différence avec la concurrence est qu'il n'y a pas d'alcool servi à bord mais ce n'est qu'un détail. En revanche, l'alcool étant interdit en Arabie saoudite il ne faut pas en acheter au duty free car on passe un contrôle lors de l'escale (même en restant dans la zone internationale), ceux qui en avaient acheté par manque d'information se le sont vu confisquer. Le tout est de le savoir avant.

Bref nous reprendrons cette compagnie les yeux fermés!
translated_text: Madrid Bangkok with family. We were a bit worried given the reviews, but the prices were much lower than the competition, so we validated the flights (Madrid - Jeddah - Bangkok), that is, 4 round-trip flights. I specify that we are a family of 4 (2 adults and 2 children) and are used to long-haul flights with several airlines (Air Caraïbes, Air France, Oman Air, Turkish Airlines,...). Well, we have nothing to complain about! First of all, all 4 flights were on time (already a good point). The onboard service was very good: a small towel for hands at the entrance, a small fabric pouch with eye mask, headphones, toothbrush..., decent meals, very pleasant staff who regularly offered glasses of water, a decent choice of films,... in short, a classic economy class. We read here and there that shorts were prohibited; that's false! On the return from Thailand, many people traveled in shorts. The only difference with the competition is that no alcohol is served on board, but that's just a detail. However, since alcohol is prohibited in Saudi Arabia, you shouldn't buy it at duty-free because there is a check during the layover (even while remaining in the international zone); those who had bought it due to lack of information had it confiscated. The key is to know this in advance. In short, we will definitely fly with this airline again!

raw_text:
Disappointing Experience with Saudia Airlines – A Letdown for Travelers with Medical Needs I recently had an extremely disappointing experience with Saudia Airlines, which I feel compelled to share—especially for those with chronic conditions like diabetes who rely on basic medical accommodations while traveling.

As a diabetic traveler with over 45 years of flying experience, I have never encountered such a lack of support, empathy, and professionalism from an airline. Unlike carriers such as Emirates, which have always accommodated my medical needs without issue, Saudia refused to place my insulin in the onboard fridge. Instead, they handed me a paper bag with loose ice cubes, expecting me to soak my insulin vials and pens in melting ice and hold them throughout the flight—an unhygienic, impractical, and medically irresponsible solution.

This is especially shocking given that Saudi Arabia has one of the highest diabetes prevalence rates in the world and is actively working toward Vision 2030, positioning itself as a modern, world-class tourism hub. If the country wants to be at the forefront of global hospitality and attract international visitors, its flagship airline must do better.

To make matters worse, the cabin crew was unhelpful, dismissive, and outright rude when I asked for assistance. Their lack of compassion and awareness was unacceptable, particularly for an airline that should uphold the highest standards of service.

Beyond this, the food options were subpar—heavily processed, full of sugar, and lacking in healthy alternatives, making it even more unsuitable for travelers with dietary restrictions.

If you have a chronic medical condition and rely on airlines for even basic accommodations, think twice before flying with Saudia. They clearly do not prioritize passenger well-being, inclusivity, or global service standards.

Frankly, Saudia Airlines needs a serious overhaul in management and policies to meet international standards. Until then, I strongly recommend choosing a different airline if you value professionalism, support, and a seamless travel experience.

raw_text:
A Wonderful Experience Flying with Saudia for My First Umrah I recently flew with Saudia from Kuala Lumpur to Jeddah. This was my first time traveling with Saudia and also my first time performing Umrah, so I was both excited and nervous, not knowing what to expect. However, Saudia made the journey a truly pleasant and comforting experience.

Mid-flight, I developed a headache and asked one of the crew members, Dahlia, if she had Panadol. She went out of her way to find it for me and even followed up later to check if I was feeling better. Her kindness and sincerity stood out for me.

What I appreciated most was how well the entire experience aligned with the spiritual nature of the journey. Since most passengers were fellow pilgrims, there was a strong sense of shared purpose. The crew understood our needs perfectly—especially since it was Ramadan and we were crossing time zones. They carefully timed the meals for Suhoor, announced when we reached the Miqat area, and provided the kind of thoughtful support that truly makes a difference.

If you’re performing Hajj or Umrah, I highly recommend flying with Saudia. The level of care and understanding they provide makes the journey feel seamless and spiritually uplifting. You wouldn’t want it any other way!

raw_text:
Do not recommend From the moment I sat down, I noticed that the headrest portion of my seat was broken, with exposed metal that made it extremely uncomfortable and painful. I immediately notified a flight attendant, but I was told that I had no choice but to remain in that seat as there were no available alternatives.

During takeoff, my head kept hitting the metal, causing discomfort and pain. Given that this was a 13-hour flight, I naturally wanted to rest, but this was impossible due to the broken headrest. When I raised the issue again with another flight attendant, she attempted to place a cushion over it and instructed me not to touch or move it—a completely unrealistic solution for a long-haul flight.

Realizing the severity of the issue, I requested to speak with a supervisor. After attempting to adjust the seat himself and acknowledging the problem, he finally moved me to another seat. However, this relief was short-lived as that seat belonged to another passenger, and I was forced to return to my original broken seat before descent.

This entire ordeal was unacceptable and completely mishandled:

There was no empathy from the cabin crew for the pain and discomfort I endured.

I was forced to move back and forth, adding unnecessary stress to an already difficult situation.

I was separated from my daughter, making the experience even more frustrating.

Ultimately, I had to endure one of the worst flight experiences of my life due to a broken seat that should never have been assigned to a passenger in the first place.

raw_text: Horrible Flight was delayed for 2 hours and 1/2 with no communication to the passengers. We were stuck on the airplane for the entire time with no valid reasoning from anyone. A fight broke out amongst the passengers and everyone was complaining. After the arrival of the police, the fighting passengers were escorted out, yet the plane didn't take off or announce any updates to everyone. When I got up to complain after over 2 hours and get answers from the crew, i was just told to get back to my seat. I refused and let them know that if we were not in the air in the next 15 minutes, me and the passengers were going to leave the aircraft. Shortly after we arrived to Cairo but with the police awaiting me and a couple passengers for merely just voicing our concerns to the crew. This is unacceptable as everyone on the plane was frustrated yet in the end we were the ones painted as the problem. I have videos and pictures from this scene for the record and I will never recommend this airline!

Save Your Money – Worst Airline Experience Such an AWFUL airline. Honestly, they only care about squeezing money out of passengers, not about comfort or basic decency. A short trip from Dammam to Abha cost nearly 9,000 SAR for 5 people (including 2 special needs kids!) while flying to Egypt is about 5,000 SAR. Make it make sense.

On top of the outrageous pricing, the experience itself was terrible. The legroom was awful definitely not made for taller or overweight passengers and the seats were so uncomfortable they caused back pain. To make matters worse, even basic things like water weren’t free. They charged 4 riyals for a tiny bottle of water… seriously?

raw_text:
Unprofessional response - not good for Business Travels I am writing to formally express my disappointment regarding the handling of my recent travel experience with Flyadeal.

Despite having checked in my luggage as per standard procedures, it was not delivered to my destination. This caused significant inconvenience and disruption to my plans, as I am a Field Engineer and from arrival airport, I was having further 3 hours travel by road to NEOM, carrying my PPEs, clothing stuff and my medicines.

What has been particularly frustrating is the lack of communication following this incident. On registering my complain to airline they have responded with a reason that because I did not board flight, that’s why luggage was not sent, however in my complain email, I have also attached my luggage tag.

After that I have reached out via email on multiple occasions seeking updates and assistance but have not received any meaningful response or resolution.

After my luggage was not delivered with a reason that I was not board, to my destination, I sent an email to your team prior to my return journey, requesting that—if the luggage had not yet been dispatched in these 2 days—it be held at the origin airport so I could collect it upon my return. Unfortunately, despite this clear instruction, my luggage was still sent to the previous destination, causing further inconvenience and delay.

This absence of follow-up reflects poorly on the level of professionalism and customer care I expected from your airline.

I would appreciate an explanation for the delay in communication and the steps your team is taking to ensure such incidents are handled more effectively in the future.

I trust you will treat this matter with the seriousness it deserves and look forward to your prompt response.
Sincerely,
Shoaib Ismail
(Flyadeal Traveller)

Formal Complaint Regarding Incorrect Baggage Information and Unprofessional Treatment – Booking via Wego / Flyadeal I am writing to formally file a complaint regarding a deeply disappointing experience I had during my recent travel, which was booked through your platform (Wego) with Flyadeal Airlines.

The ticket I purchased clearly included a checked baggage allowance, as confirmed in the booking details provided by Wego. However, upon reaching the Flyadeal check-in counter, I was informed that no baggage was included with my ticket. Despite presenting the booking confirmation that explicitly stated the baggage allowance, the staff at the counter insisted I pay an additional fee.

Not only was this a case of miscommunication and misinformation, but the conduct of the Flyadeal staff was also unprofessional. Instead of reviewing the situation reasonably, they argued with me and pressured me into paying for a baggage allowance that was already part of my booking. This caused considerable inconvenience and frustration, especially when the error was not on my part.

Booking Reference number is: E8RGPW

worst airline in the GCC Flying with Flynas has been the worst travel experience I’ve ever had. From start to finish, everything was handled poorly. Their website and app constantly glitch, making even basic tasks like check-in nearly impossible. On top of that, the payment system is unreliable—I received error messages claiming “payment pending” even though the booking was already processed, and I was left stranded unable to check in.

Customer service is just as bad, with unhelpful staff who either pass you around or provide no solutions at all. Delays are treated as normal, with no proper updates or compensation, and the overall organization is a mess. The seats are uncomfortable, flights are often late, and the entire experience feels chaotic and unprofessional.

If you value your time, money, and peace of mind, avoid Flynas at all costs. They are unreliable, disorganized, and easily the worst airline I’ve dealt with.

Nein Danke Eine vermutlich nach Sharia Regeln geführte Fluggesellschaft. Dieses fast "Nonstop Praying" war all gegenwärtig. Das Essen ist bestimmt toll für islamisch/arabische Gäste. Striktes Alkoholverbot. Merkwürdiger Zimt Kaffee. Landung Bali war grenzwertig. Zu früh und zu hart aufgesetzt. Das hat ordentlich gerumpelt. Der gesamte Flug war geprägt durch endlose Kontrollen (7x). Es wurde mir erst in der letzten Minute(bording lief bereits und war fast abgeschlossen) ein Sitzplatz zugewiesen. Nach 2 Stunden reklamieren.
Fazit = nie wieder!
translated_text: No thanks A presumably Sharia law-run airline. This almost 'nonstop praying' was omnipresent. The food is probably great for Islamic/Arab guests. Strict alcohol ban. Strange cinnamon coffee. The landing in Bali was borderline. Too early and too hard touchdown. It was quite bumpy. The entire flight was marked by endless checks (7 times). I was only assigned a seat at the last minute (boarding was already underway and almost finished) after 2 hours of complaining. Conclusion = never again!

raw_text:
Unprofesstional & Worst Support Team - Inappropriate Boarding Time and No Announcement at the Boarding Gate. One of the Worst Experience i have ever had with any Airline.

I am Writing this review to formally inform all to go through the Reviews before planning to travel with flyadeal Airline, I would like to bring to your notice regarding an issue I encountered with flight F3 519 from Riyadh to Dubai, scheduled for departure at 11:25 PM on 6th May 2025.

After completing immigration formalities at Riyadh airport, I was waiting near Gate 401, adjacent to the gate lobby area, due to limited seating. Four other passengers, Emirati nationals booked on the same flight, were also waiting in the same area. The boarding time was 10:40 PM, and we remained in close proximity to the gate, expecting an announcement. Unfortunately, no boarding calls or updates were announced in our area.

When I approached the gate to check if there is any delay in scheduled flight timing, I was informed by staff that there was no delay in flight schedule, that I was late, and that the flight was ready to depart. Despite my repeated requests for assistance, the staff did not offer any support. I later visited the information desk, where I met the same four passengers who reported identical experiences, confirming they also missed the flight due to no announcements. It is highly unlikely that five passengers, all present near the gate, would collectively miss boarding without any fault.

Given these circumstances, I raised the Complaint to flyadeal requesting compensation for this inconvenience but the support team came back to me mentioning as per attached.

After this i thought of checking the Review on tripadvisor.com. and the very first review I found was exactly similar to my situation. The support team is Unprofessional, they don't even try to understand and investigate in proper way, instead they are protecting their Boarding Gate Team Members lying to the passengers.

I will not travel anymore with this airline even if they publish any good offers as they are not reliable.

Further, I will not recommend any of my Family and Friends to travel with flyadeal

Regards,
Sarfraz Sheik Kareem

⭐☆☆☆☆ Extremely Disappointed with Flynas – No Zamzam Water Allowed I recently flew with Flynas and was extremely disappointed to find out that they do not allow Zamzam water to be carried, even though it is a common and sacred item for many Muslim travelers returning from Umrah or Hajj.

This policy is incredibly disrespectful and inconsiderate, especially given the significance of Zamzam water in Islamic tradition. Most other airlines, including budget ones, make accommodations for passengers to carry at least 5 liters of Zamzam. Flynas, on the other hand, completely prohibits it – without providing any clear or compassionate explanation.

The staff were unhelpful and dismissive when I tried to inquire about this policy. For an airline that operates heavily within the Muslim world, this is unacceptable. I will never fly Flynas again and would not recommend it to anyone performing pilgrimage or traveling from Saudi Arabia with the hope of carrying Zamzam.

Flynas – you need to rethink your policies and show some basic respect to your customers’ religious values.

raw_text:
Disappointed with Cancellation Policy – Hoping for Better Customer Support Disappointed with Cancellation Policy – Hoping for Better Customer Support

I recently booked a Flynas flight from Dubai to Jeddah but unfortunately had to cancel my trip due to not being able to secure a Saudi visa. I reached out to Flynas customer service to request a waiver or at least partial refund due to these genuine and unforeseen circumstances.

However, I received a very brief response stating that “reservations in economic categories do not accept cancellation” and that the full value of the ticket would be lost. No flexibility, no discussion of options, and no human engagement — just a one-line, auto-generated response.

I find this approach disappointing, especially when the reason for cancellation is beyond the passenger's control. As a loyal regional traveller, I expect more empathy and customer care, particularly from an airline that is striving to establish itself as a leading low-cost carrier.

I have formally requested that my case be escalated, and I genuinely hope Flynas takes this feedback seriously and reconsiders their stance. I am still open to a fair and goodwill-based resolution. Good customer service is not just about policies — it’s about how you handle exceptions with care.

Booking Reference:
Case Number: CAS-2505170006

Terrible Experience with Saudia Airlines – Avoid If You Can! I don’t usually write bad reviews, but my experience with Saudia Airlines flight SV117 from Jeddah to London Heathrow was so disappointing that I feel compelled to warn others.

The flight was delayed by over three hours without any proper updates or explanations. Once onboard, the service was just as bad. Meal service was extremely slow, and by the time the food reached us, they had completely run out of meat options—leaving only vegetables. This is unacceptable on a long-haul flight where passengers expect a proper meal choice.

Because of the delay, I missed my connecting flight from Heathrow to Manchester, leaving me stranded at the airport with my four children for seven hours. To make things worse, there were no alternative flights available, forcing me to spend an extra £300 on bus tickets just to get home. We didn’t arrive until the next day, exhausted and frustrated.

To top it all off, I filed a complaint with the airline, but they haven’t even bothered to respond. The complete lack of customer care and responsibility is shocking.

This was one of the worst travel experiences I’ve ever had, and I absolutely do not recommend Saudia Airlines to anyone. If you value reliability, service, and customer support, stay away from this airline.

raw_text: Saudia staff highly irresponsible for their actions and totally ignoring customers' rights. Our inbound trip from Europe included a domestic flight Riyadh to Jeddah en route to Jakarta. As a precaution, I had previously contacted the Saudia office in Jakarta to know if any transit visa was due at Saudi airports, and the reply was that we did not need any. Thanks to this wrong information given by Saudia representatives, we were forced to pay a hefty 106 USD each for a tourist visa at Riyadh immigration counters. No possibility to contact Saudia, no chance to argue: stuck there unless the tourist visas worth 318 USD were paid. Once I managed to contact the Saudia staff in Jeddah, it was clear that any reimbursement request should go through Saudia website. Travel I recently had an extremely frustrating and disappointing experience flying with Saudi Airlines, which I feel compelled to share – especially for families traveling with disabled members.

We were subjected to an unexpected 24-hour layover, with little to no explanation or support from the airline staff. This alone would have been bad enough, but the treatment of my disabled son made the experience unbearable.

Despite clearly informing the airline ahead of time about his condition and need for wheelchair assistance, they took the wheelchair away from us, leaving him stranded and forced to walk long distances through the terminal. The nearest toilet was extremely far, and no one from the staff seemed to care or make any attempt to accommodate him, even after repeated requests. There was a shocking lack of basic human compassion or cooperation.

Not once did any staff member come to check on us, offer assistance, or apologize for the situation. We were left to fend for ourselves in an unfamiliar airport for an entire day, while trying to care for a disabled child without the most basic accessibility support.

This level of negligence and lack of care is completely unacceptable for any airline, let alone one that promotes itself as a provider of international service. I would strongly advise anyone traveling with elderly or disabled passengers to avoid Saudi Airlines at all costs.

There was no communication, no support, and no empathy – just complete disregard for our situation.

raw_text: WARNING WORST AIRLINE EVER. AVOID IF YOU DONT WANT TO WASTE MONEY!!! Worst airline i have EVER experienced. I usually fly with emirates and i have no issues at all. This is my first time travelling with saudi airlines and i never expected this kind of behaviour for a country so strict like this. We were flying from Istanbul back to Nairobi, with a transit at Jeddah. When we boarded the plane an arab lady reclined her seat even before taking off. I asked her kindly to move her seat back up since i had no space. She just looked at me, and went back to scrolling on her phone. It shows you that Saudi people are very rascist when they see other nationalities coming into their country. Staff was very rude, using aggressive force to give you food trays, we also asked for an extra blanket, and the attendant just threw the bag at us. Many people are complaining about saudia airlines. On our flight from jeddah to nairobi, they sprayed a harmless insecticide only when we were travelling to africa. When i arrived in nairobi, i found 2 of my three bags, but i was unable to find one of them. We looked up and down for it but we couldnt find it. We spent almost 3 hours looking for the lost bag until we reported it to baggage claim. They delayed our flight going to jeddah and we had barely one hour to go otherwise we would miss it. Terrible and racist experience.

raw_text:
Unacceptable Service and Racism on Saudi Airlines: A Nightmare Experience Where should I even start? Traveling with an infant and a child is already challenging enough, but Saudi Airlines made it even more difficult. At the check-in counter, I was handed boarding passes with seats assigned to different positions on the plane for each family member, despite selecting adjacent seats when I originally booked the tickets. I paid extra for a higher-tier plan so I could choose better seats upfront, but when I asked about the change, I was told the plane had been switched, and that’s why the seats were altered. How is that even possible?

I was then redirected to the customer relations center, where the staff was incredibly unhelpful. Without even listening to our situation, they bluntly told me they couldn’t do anything. Meanwhile, my app still showed the seats I had originally selected, yet they refused to assist, advising me instead to ask five passengers to switch seats—an absurd and unreasonable suggestion.

The onboard experience was equally disappointing. The cabin crew was the worst I’ve ever encountered on any international airline. They were unfriendly, uncooperative, and lacked basic professionalism. The call button for assistance worked, but no one responded. If you wanted a cup of water, you had to go up to them every single time; they wouldn’t come to you. On a 13-hour flight, we were only served two meals, and though a snack was prepared, only a handful of passengers received it. They claimed there wasn’t enough to go around. It was clear that certain passengers were being selectively served, with smiles and proper attention, while others were ignored.

If you’re a person of color who doesn’t speak Arabic, I strongly advise you to avoid this airline. The level of racism and outright resignation toward passengers’ needs was shocking, and it felt like it was completely normal to them. This was a deeply frustrating and disrespectful experience that I never want to repeat.

raw_text:
Misleading Flight Duration, Schedule Change Without Notice, and Poor Customer Service ★☆☆☆☆ Worst Airline Experience – Misleading Flight Info & Rude Customer Service

I booked a Flynas flight from Dubai to Amman thinking it was a 5-hour 45-minute journey with a short stop in Riyadh – exactly as shown on their official website. That’s the only reason I booked this flight. But after booking, Flynas changed the second leg of the flight (Riyadh to Amman) without adjusting the first leg. Suddenly, I was stuck with a 10-hour trip including a 5+ hour layover!

I called their customer service, and it was a nightmare. I spoke with an agent named Nasir Abdullah for over 30 minutes, trying to explain the issue. He kept repeating the same thing: “You were told the departure and arrival times.” That’s not the point! I specifically asked about flight duration before booking, and their agent said “5 hours.” That was false.

When I asked for a free flight change (which is allowed once according to their policy), he refused and said “you agreed to it.” No, I didn’t agree to a 10-hour journey — it was never mentioned until after the ticket was issued!

The worst part? Their website still shows the wrong flight duration, misleading more customers.

❌ Misleading booking info
❌ Poor handling of flight changes
❌ Zero accountability
❌ Rude and unhelpful customer service

This was my first and last time flying with Flynas. Completely unprofessional and disrespectful of passengers’ time and trust.

Vuelo mediocre, buscaria otras opciones El personal fue muy amable y nos ayudo en todo momento en ambos vuelos (Hicimos escala en Jeddah), el problema fue el propio avion.
En el primer vuelo no funcionaba el wifi, problema menor, y el vuelo se retraso una media hora, dejandonos poco margen para el enlace, aunque también este se retraso.
En el segundo vuelo fue aun peor, no funcionaban las pantallas para ver peliculas y tampoco algunos enchufes (incluidos los nuestros) asi que no podiamos hacer mas que leer durante 8 horas ya que los asientos eran bastante incomodos (Como los de todas las aerolineas)
translated_text: Mediocre flight, I would look for other options. The staff was very kind and helped us at all times on both flights (we had a stopover in Jeddah). The problem was the plane itself. On the first flight, the wifi did not work, a minor problem, and the flight was delayed by about half an hour, leaving us little margin for the connection, although it was also delayed. On the second flight, it was even worse; the screens to watch movies did not work and some power outlets (including ours) did not work, so we could only read for 8 hours as the seats were quite uncomfortable (like all airlines).

raw_text: Des voleurs qui vous rient au nez Compagnie et personnel catastrophiques. Notre vol a été annulé et nous n’avons eu aucune explication à l’aéroport de Riyad. Un membre du personnel nous a fait payer un nouveau billet pour plus de 450.- chacun et nous a obligé à payer en cash et a ensuite mis les billets dans sa poche… de plus il avait l’air content de la situation et en rigolait avec ses collègues. Son niveau d’anglais était catastrophique et ses explications l’étaient tout autant. Nous avons fait le ping-pong entre plusieurs membres du personnel à l’aéroport pour obtenir des explications qui ne sont jamais venues. Le PIRE dans tout cela est qu’il n’y a aucun moyen de les contacter car Saudi Airlines n’a pas de mail. Tout se fait sur leur site internet ou via leur application et les demandes n’aboutissent jamais et vous n’obtiendrez aucune réponse. A éviter absolument !
translated_text: Thieves who laugh in your face. Company and staff are catastrophic. Our flight was canceled and we had no explanation at Riyadh airport. A staff member made us pay for a new ticket for more than 450 each and forced us to pay in cash and then put the tickets in his pocket... he even seemed happy about the situation and laughed with his colleagues. His English level was catastrophic and so were his explanations. We were bounced between several staff members at the airport to get explanations that never came. The WORST part is that there is no way to contact them because Saudi Airlines has no email. Everything is done on their website or via their app and requests never succeed and you get no response. Absolutely avoid!

raw_text:
Complaint about Riyadh Airport and Missed Flight to Karachi – October 2 Complaint about Riyadh Airport and Missed Flight to Karachi – October 23rd

On October 23rd, I had a connecting flight from Medina to Riyadh, with my final destination to Karachi. We arrived in Riyadh on time, but the staff made us sit outside in a bus, for about half an hour. Then, they made us go through the immigration process again, which caused unnecessary delay.

By the time we reached the gate, only 15 minutes were left before departure, but they had already closed the gate and did not allow us to board — even though we all had valid boarding passes. There were 18 passengers in total, all traveling to Karachi, left behind. Shockingly, the flight remained on the ground for nearly an hour, yet they still refused to let us on board.

The staff at Riyadh airport were extremely unhelpful and unprofessional. No one tried to assist us or even listen to our situation. It felt as if they had no regard for passengers or their responsibilities. This was my first time flying through Riyadh airport, and it was a very disappointing experience — especially after completing my Umrah.

As a U.S. citizen and a fellow Muslim, I felt deeply disrespected and hurt by the way we were treated. I sincerely hope the concerned authorities take notice of this issue and ensure that no other passengers have to face such behavior in the future.

raw_text:
Terrible experience On Saturday, June 14th Mm wife and I were traveling in Business Class, and upon arrival, we were informed that one of our bags was missing.
The ground staff at Riyadh Airport assured me that the baggage would be delivered to our home address in Al-Qassim (Al-Rass) after filing a Report (RUHSV70688). At no point was I informed of any restrictions regarding delivery locations. In fact, when I confirmed with your team on Sunday, June 15th, they reiterated that the baggage would be sent directly to our home.
However, I was shocked to receive a call on Monday, June 16th, demanding that I personally collect the baggage from Al-Qassim Airport (which is 80 km away from our home) or arrange for someone else to do so. This is completely unreasonable because:
1. No prior warning was given about delivery limitations.
2. Had I known, I would have preferred to collect it from Riyadh Airport, which would have been far more convenient.
3. The delay and inconvenience were caused by your airline’s error, and I should not bear the burden of resolving it.
Today I completed 4 days and still waiting for my bag to be delivered!

Bad experience Dear Saudi Airlines Customer Service,

I am writing to formally raise a complaint regarding my recent travel experience with your airline related to my Hajj flight on the 28 05 2025, which caused me significant emotional and financial distress.

My flight was booked as part of a Hajj package costing £1,914. I arrived at the airport 1 hour and 30 minutes before departure, which I understood to be within the acceptable check-in and boarding window. However, to my surprise, the boarding gate had already closed. Furthermore, the staff at the gate were unhelpful and dismissive in their manner, which made an already difficult situation even worse.

I was later informed that my ticket was a "group ticket," yet I was booked to fly from London, while all my fellow group members were booked to fly from Manchester Airport. If this was indeed a group booking, I would like to understand why I was separated from the group and not assigned to the same departure airport. This discrepancy is critical because it seems to have contributed directly to the problems I faced.

Given the religious and emotional importance of Hajj, this experience has been especially disheartening. I am requesting a full investigation into the following:

1. Why was I booked from a different airport if this was truly a group ticket?

2. Why was the gate closed so early despite me arriving at the airport 1.5 hours in advance?

3. Why was the staff at the gate unhelpful and rude in dealing with my situation?

I kindly request compensation for the flight I missed and for the distress caused, along with a clear explanation of the booking process and responsibility.

I hope you will treat this matter with the seriousness it deserves and respond promptly.

Sincerely,

raw_text:
Saudia Airlines - positiv überrascht Nachdem ich soviel negatives über die Saudia gehört habe, muss ich hier meine Rezession schreiben. Ich kann die negativen Bewertungen nicht verstehen.
Meine Erfahrungen mit Saudia waren nur positiv.
Wir hatten eine leichte Verspätung beim Abflug, die fast komplett wieder eingeholt wurde.
Das Personal war zuvorkommend, freundlich und hilfsbereit. Es gab sogar Präsente für die Kinder.
Es gab auf jedem Flug (Genf-Dschidda, Dschidda-Dubai und zurück) jeweils eine warme Mahlzeit, genügend zu trinken und sogar Obst und Chips.
Die Sondermahlzeiten die wir bestellt hatten, waren vorrätig und gut.
Die Sitze sind groß und ich konnte, bei einer Körpergröße von 188cm, sogar meine Beine fast ausstrecken. Selbst die Sitzbreite sind für korpulentere Menschen gut geeignet. Das hatte ich bei Condor oder anderen Billigairlines nicht.
Das Entertainmentprogramm war international. Es gab Blockbuster in deutsch (Dune 2, John Wick)
Unser Gepäck kam ohne Beschädigung sicher in Dubai und zurück an (mit Zwischenstopp von nur 1,5h in Dschidda).
Bei Turbulenzen wurde umgehend die Flughöhe angepasst um in ruhigere Luftschichten zu gelangen.
Die Sitzplatzreservierung ist auf der App einfacher als auf der Homepage.
Alles in allem eine gute Airline die ich empfehlen kann.
translated_text: Saudia Airlines - positively surprised After hearing so many negative things about Saudia, I have to write my review here. I cannot understand the negative reviews. My experiences with Saudia were only positive. We had a slight delay at departure, which was almost completely made up. The staff was courteous, friendly, and helpful. There were even gifts for the children. On every flight (Geneva-Jeddah, Jeddah-Dubai, and back) there was a warm meal, plenty to drink, and even fruit and chips. The special meals we ordered were available and good. The seats are large and I, at a height of 188cm, could almost stretch my legs out. Even the seat width is suitable for larger people. I did not have that with Condor or other low-cost airlines. The entertainment program was international. There were blockbusters in German (Dune 2, John Wick). Our luggage arrived safely in Dubai and back without damage (with only a 1.5-hour stopover in Jeddah). During turbulence, the flight altitude was immediately adjusted to reach calmer air layers. Seat reservation is easier on the app than on the homepage. All in all, a good airline that I can recommend.

raw_text: Gerne jederzeit wieder Saudia Airlines Auch ich kann die schlechten Bewertungen für Saudia Airlines nicht nachvollziehen. Wir sind mit einer halbstündigen Verspätung gestartet, welche aber so reingeholt wurde, dass wir in Riad sogar früher angekommen sind. Das Durchchecken des Gepäcks weiter nach Bangkok hat einwandfrei funktioniert und auch der Aufenthalt in Riad war angenehm. Bei beiden Flügen hatten wir einen wirklich guten Service, sehr freundliches Personal, sehr gutes Essen, sehr bequeme Sitze und ein sehr gutes Entertainment Programm. Auch auf deutsch. Auch unser Rückflug, wieder mit Zwischenstopp in Riad, hat problemlos geklappt und auch hier wieder sehr freundliches hilfsbereites Personal und ein guter Service. Ich würde jederzeit wieder mit Saudia Airlines fliegen! Das Preis-Leistungsverhältnis ist einfach top!
translated_text: Gladly anytime again Saudia Airlines I also cannot understand the bad reviews for Saudia Airlines. We started with a half-hour delay, which was caught up so much that we even arrived earlier in Riyadh. The through-checking of luggage further to Bangkok worked perfectly and the stay in Riyadh was pleasant. On both flights, we had really good service, very friendly staff, very good food, very comfortable seats, and a very good entertainment program. Also in German. Our return flight, again with a stopover in Riyadh, also went smoothly and again very friendly helpful staff and good service. I would fly with Saudia Airlines anytime again! The price-performance ratio is simply top!

raw_text: Teures Zusatzgepäck Saudi Arabia Airlines lässt sich alles extra bezahlen. Es gibt z. B. keine Möglichkeit, um Sportgepäck "kostengünstig" zu buchen. Stattdessen muss man teuer Zusatzgepäck (300 € für 23 kg hin und zurück) bezahlen und für jedes weitere Kilo nochmal ca. 30 Euro zusätzlich. Auf meine Bitte nach kulanter Vorgehensweise wurde gesagt, 3 kg wären 3 kg. Letztendlich bezahle ich nun für 26 kg zusätzliches Gepäck knapp 400 Euro mehr. Dafür ist man von 1 - 2 Jahren noch die komplette Strecke hin und zurück geflogen. Tut mir leid für alle Fluggäste mit einem BMI > 30, aber ihr belastet den Kerosinverbrauch etwas mehr als meine 3 kg Übergepäck. Ich als Verbraucher finde, dass es irgendwann auch mal genug ist.
translated_text: Expensive extra baggage. Saudi Arabia Airlines charges extra for everything. For example, there is no option to book sports equipment 'cheaply'. Instead, you have to pay expensive extra baggage fees (300 € for 23 kg round trip) and about 30 euros extra for each additional kilo. When I asked for leniency, they said 3 kg is 3 kg. In the end, I am now paying nearly 400 euros more for 26 kg of extra baggage. For 1-2 years, I flew the entire route round trip. Sorry for all passengers with a BMI > 30, but you consume a bit more kerosene than my 3 kg of excess baggage. As a consumer, I think enough is enough at some point.

raw_text:
Three-day flight delay and Bad customer care behavior I booked a flight for my wife from Jeddah to Calicut (via Riyadh) on April 28, 2025. We arrived at the airport on time, and she obtained her boarding pass. After I left, she completed all procedures and waited to board the 8:25 pm flight to Riyadh.

One hour before departure, a Flynas staff member informed her the flight was rescheduled to 10:15 pm and that they would rebook her to ensure she caught her connection. They escorted her and many other passengers to the Flynas office near the boarding gates, collected everyone’s boarding passes, and left them waiting outside with no updates or amenities. After 30–40 minutes, they issued new boarding passes dated May 1 three days later without consulting her. When she explained that her Umrah visa expires on April 29 per Ministry of Hajj regulations, they showed no concern.

Next, they directed her to collect her luggage from the ground floor arraival section , where she waited over 90 minutes and was treated dismissively. In the meantime, I returned to the airport, having learned of the three day rebooking. Before I arrived, Flynas had arranged hotel accommodation for affected passengers but attempted to put her in a shared room with strangers. I insisted on a single room, and after some argument they relented; the food provided was nevertheless poor.

The following morning she checked out, since the Flynas supervisor made it clear they would not assume any responsibility for visa expiry penalties leaving the passenger liable. The ground staff and supervisors were uniformly rude and indifferent. As a result, we booked a different Airline flight on April 29, and she departed on time.

When I asked about canceling the unused boarding pass and obtaining a refund, they again refused and treated us brusquely. then informed the travel agency to request it by mail and still didn't get reply and we are waiting for that. Based on this experience, I strongly advise against choosing Flynas: opt instead for a more reliable airline to ensure a safe, stress-free journey.

raw_text:
Chatbot and other Refundmaster 🤡 Die Fluggesellschaft an sich ist unserer Meinung nach, solange Du im Flugzeug bist gut und freundlich. Von Service, über Essen bis hin zum Flugzeug an sich hatten wir da eigentlich keine Probleme.

Wenn du dich jedoch um eine Rückerstattung kümmern willst, da dein Flug gecancelt wurde, wünsche ich dir viel Glück.
Auf dem Weg dorthin kommst du nämlich an einem Hamsterrad aus Chatbot und falschen Mailadressen vorbei, auch wenn der Bot recht „freundlich“ erscheint lässt er dich das Karussell aus Booking Nr und 239791 Eingaben round and round fahren und du kannst bei vollem Betrieb aussteigen/ aufgeben, das ist natürlich dir überlassen denn dafür kann die Fluggesellschaft ja nichts wenn Du einfach aussteigst ;). Kannst natürlich auch sitzen bleiben und deine Zeit verschwenden, denn an dein Ziel wirst du hierbei nicht kommen.
Liegt vermutlich daran da das EU- Recht hier nicht gilt, schlecht für dich. Achja, oder probiers über die wundervolle App. Auf den Bilder sieht man wies abläuft. Kannst dann entweder mit Indien, Spanien oder Saudi Arabien telefonieren. Helfen tut dir keiner. Und vergesse nicht die Unkosten die durchs telefonieren entstehen anzuhängen, die werden dir sicher auch erstattet.

Witzig ist auch das Sie unseren Flug einfach storniert haben obwohl er, wie wir dann festgestellt haben, abgeflogen ist!? Noch nie erlebt sowas, frage mich wo das Problem lag? Für uns bedeutete das Hotel und zig Umbuchungen/ Stornierungen unserer weiterreise, wo wir jetzt auch die Nudel am dampfen haben da wenns ums Geld geht natürlich keiner erstatten will. Korrigiere.. erstatten kann. Weil man will ja eigentlich, aaaber…

Traurig, traurig.. aber hey der umgebuchte Flug war auch nett und hatte lediglich 10h Verspätung. Pariser Flughafen war toll..

dennoch würden wir wieder bei euch buchen da uns der Service echt gefallen hat, aber bitte nehmt uns einfach jedes mal wie geplant mit und kümmert euch um einen Internationalen Service bei Problemen.

Zum Schluss noch ein großen Dank an die beiden Servicemitarbeiter, welche uns mit ihrer wertvollen Arbeit möglichst schnell aus der Leitung kickten.

Guten Flug
translated_text: Chatbot and other Refundmaster 🤡 The airline itself is, in our opinion, good and friendly as long as you are on the plane. From service to food to the plane itself, we actually had no problems. However, if you want to take care of a refund because your flight was canceled, I wish you good luck. On the way there, you will encounter a hamster wheel of chatbots and incorrect email addresses, even if the bot seems quite 'friendly', it will make you go round and round with booking numbers and 239791 entries, and you can get off/quit at full operation, that is of course up to you because the airline can't do anything if you just get off ;). You can also stay seated and waste your time, because you won't get to your destination this way. This is probably because EU law does not apply here, bad for you. Oh, or try the wonderful app. The pictures show how it goes. You can then either call India, Spain, or Saudi Arabia. No one will help you. And don't forget to attach the costs incurred from the phone calls, they will surely be reimbursed as well. It's funny that they simply canceled our flight even though it, as we later found out, had departed!? Never experienced anything like this, I wonder where the problem was? For us, this meant hotel and numerous rebookings/cancellations of our onward journey, where we are now also steaming because when it comes to money, of course, no one wants to reimburse. Correct me.. they can reimburse. Because they actually want to, but… Sad, sad.. but hey, the rebooked flight was also nice and only had a 10-hour delay. Paris airport was great.. still, we would book with you again because we really liked the service, but please just take us with you as planned every time and take care of international service in case of problems. Finally, a big thank you to the two service employees who quickly kicked us off the line with their valuable work. Have a good flight.

raw_text:
Saudia Airlines Business Class – A Complete Disappointment I recently flew from London to Dhaka to London with Saudia Airlines and upgraded to business class for the Dhaka to Jeddah and Jeddah to Dhaka leg of my journey. Unfortunately, the experience was far from what was promised and turned out to be one of the worst airline experiences I’ve ever had.

Check-in Chaos
Saudia Airlines advertises a dedicated check-in counter for business class passengers, yet at Dhaka airport, the business class counter was completely unmanned. Meanwhile, economy passengers were being checked in smoothly. I had to wait 20 minutes, speak to multiple staff members, and go through unnecessary hassle just to complete my check-in. This delay was unacceptable, especially considering I paid a premium for business class service.

No Priority Boarding – Just a Long Queue
One of the key reasons for upgrading to business class is the priority boarding benefit. However, at the boarding gate, there was no separate queue or fast-track service for business class passengers. Everyone—business and economy—was forced into the same long line, waiting over an hour just to board the flight. This completely defeated the purpose of paying extra for business class, as my experience was no different from an economy passenger.

Unjustified & Humiliating Security Checks
After passing all the required airport security checks, I was shocked to find Saudia’s own flight crew conducting additional baggage and body searches at the airplane gate. I was forced to open my carry-on luggage in front of strangers, which felt humiliating and unnecessary. Airline staff are not security officers, and I question under what authority they carried out these additional checks. There was no security threat that justified such treatment. This invasive procedure felt like racial profiling and discrimination based on my nationality or skin colour.

Rude Crew & Poor Communication
The flight crew were shockingly unprofessional and rude. Worse, they struggled with basic English, making communication difficult. It is unacceptable for an international airline to have crew members who cannot effectively communicate with passengers.

Non-Functional In-Flight Entertainment (In both flights)
Adding to the disappointment, the in-flight entertainment system did not work at all throughout the journey. For a business class experience, this is simply unacceptable.

Final Verdict: A Waste of Money
I paid a significant amount to upgrade to business class, yet I received none of the premium benefits promised. From the chaotic check-in to the lack of priority boarding, unnecessary security checks, and unprofessional service, this was one of the worst flights I have ever taken.

I have demanded a full refund for my business class upgrade, and if I do not receive a response within two weeks, I will escalate the matter by filing a dispute with my credit card provider.

If you are considering flying business class with Saudia Airlines, DON’T WASTE YOUR MONEY!
