raw_text:
Is This the ‘New Saudia’? My Experience Was a Customer Service Disaster Extremely disappointed with Saudia’s overall service, both on the ground and in the air. My baggage went missing on a flight from Madinah to Kuala Lumpur in early April 2025. I filed a report at the airport and was initially able to track it—the system showed the bag had arrived in KL. However, from the second day onwards, the tracking number no longer worked and now simply says “report not found.” It has been more than two weeks with no updates, no communication, and no resolution. Absolutely unacceptable.

The in-flight service was also shocking. Meals were served only 30 minutes before landing, and mine was still frozen. When I raised a polite complaint, the crew simply walked away without any explanation or apology. This is not the kind of service one expects—let alone tolerates—on a full-service international airline.

Even worse, the ground staff at the boarding gate were utterly unprofessional. There was no special lane or consideration for families with children, and the staff were raising their voices—shouting, even—when giving instructions, including toward elderly passengers and young children. Their English was unclear, and the tone used was rude and aggressive. Basic customer service training, particularly in courteous communication, is desperately needed.

This was my first time experiencing such poor service from any airline. Saudia claims to be a 5-star airline undergoing a major transformation. But based on this experience, one really has to ask: does it truly live up to that standard?

From the moment we boarded, the entire crew was exceptionally polite, caring, and attentive. They went above and beyond to ensure the comfort and wellbeing of every passenger, and their kindness made a world of difference to our journey.

Flynas Flights: Mixed Experience with Excellent Crew Member & Unacceptable Luggage Damage My recent journey with Flynas presented a mixed bag of experiences, ranging from excellent service to concerning staff behavior and, most importantly, significant luggage damage.

On March 1st, my DWC to JED flight (PNR - HGKSHH) was generally good, though I was disappointed by the arrogant and unprofessional attitude of one flight attendant.

My connecting flights on March 10th (MED to RUH and RUH to CCJ, PNR - JC69YW) offered contrasting experiences. The MED to RUH flight was particularly marred by the highly unprofessional and disrespectful behavior of a flight attendant named Kristina. This level of service is simply unacceptable.

However, I must commend the exceptional service on the RUH to CCJ flight. An unnamed Saudi male flight attendant, well-groomed and wearing eyeglasses, provided truly outstanding care. He was incredibly polite, professional, and attentive to all passengers, especially children, making that segment of the journey genuinely pleasant. He's a true asset to Flynas.

The most pressing issue, however, occurred on my RUH to CCJ flight on March 10th: my luggage arrived at CCJ airport severely damaged. One wheel was completely broken, and the security key area was also damaged. I immediately filed a complaint at CCJ, but was told resolution could take months, which is incredibly frustrating and unacceptable.

While I appreciate the efforts of some of your crew, the unprofessional conduct of others and the significant damage to my luggage with a slow resolution process are serious concerns. Flynas needs to ensure consistent service quality and take prompt action on passenger issues like baggage claims.

#Flynas #FlightReview #CustomerService #BaggageDamage #TravelFeedback #Wayanad #Kerala #India

raw_text:
Worst ever travelling experience! My experience with Saudi Airlines was the worst of my life. The airline was lacking in all aspects, particularly in customer service and communication.

We flew to Jeddah for a connecting flight to Nairobi, only to discover upon arrival that our flight had been delayed for an additional 11 hours. This critical information was not communicated to the 400 passengers anxiously waiting for updates; instead, we had to demand information from staff who were reluctant to provide any.

From that point on, there was no support, food (came only after continued persistence), or accommodation offered to us. Many passengers, including myself, were left to sort out our own arrangements, leading to unnecessary stress, especially for families with young children. We were informed that we had to cover our own expenses for accommodation and meals, as it was "not their concern."

With no access to Wi-Fi, it was difficult to change our bookings. Fortunately, a kind shop assistant helped us connect to the staff Wi-Fi, allowing us to book another flight to Mombasa from Nairobi, as we were going to miss our original connection. This incurred significant costs, which we are now in the process of claiming back - after chasing on numerous occassions we eventually received a standard refund, but are yet to recieve a refund for the additional costs we incurred (9 months and counting).

The return flights with Saudi Airlines were not much better. An air hostess mistakenly served our vegetarian meals to other passengers and then offered us food containing meat. Additionally, my dad was given incorrect boarding pass details for our connecting flight. Thankfully, we had downloaded a digital copy of our tickets; otherwise, the situation could have been even worse.

If I could rate Saudi Airlines lower than one star, I would give them a minus. Their service was so terrible that it doesn’t deserve a rating!

raw_text:
Solicitud de reembolso y compensación por vuelo SV 228 con más de 6 horas de retraso – Referencia de reserva: OH6WUZ Solicitud de reembolso y compensación por vuelo SV 228 con más de 6 horas de retraso – Referencia de reserva: OH6WUZ

Estimados señores,

Me dirijo a ustedes para expresar mi profunda insatisfacción con el servicio recibido en el vuelo SV 228, con origen en Barcelona y destino Kuala Lumpur, correspondiente a la reserva número OH6WUZ.

Este vuelo estaba programado para salir a las 16:30, pero finalmente despegó a las 22:50, lo que representa un retraso de más de seis horas. Este nivel de retraso es completamente inaceptable y ha causado importantes perjuicios personales y económicos.

De acuerdo con la Regulación (CE) Nº 261/2004 del Parlamento Europeo, los pasajeros tienen derecho a una compensación económica y/o reembolso en caso de retrasos superiores a tres horas, salvo en circunstancias excepcionales. Hasta la fecha, no he recibido ninguna explicación clara, asistencia adecuada ni compensación por parte de la compañía.

Por todo lo anterior, exijo el reembolso completo del importe del billete, así como la compensación correspondiente según lo establecido por la normativa vigente. En caso de no recibir respuesta en un plazo razonable, me veré obligado/a a elevar la queja a las autoridades competentes en materia de aviación civil y a recurrir a plataformas especializadas en reclamaciones.

Espero una respuesta urgente y una solución inmediata a esta situación injusta.

Atentamente, Maria
translated_text: Request for refund and compensation for flight SV 228 with more than 6 hours delay – Booking reference: OH6WUZ Dear Sirs, I am writing to express my deep dissatisfaction with the service received on flight SV 228, originating from Barcelona and destined for Kuala Lumpur, corresponding to booking number OH6WUZ. This flight was scheduled to depart at 16:30 but finally took off at 22:50, representing a delay of more than six hours. This level of delay is completely unacceptable and has caused significant personal and economic damages. According to Regulation (EC) No 261/2004 of the European Parliament, passengers are entitled to financial compensation and/or reimbursement in case of delays exceeding three hours, except in exceptional circumstances. To date, I have not received any clear explanation, adequate assistance, or compensation from the company. For all the above reasons, I demand a full refund of the ticket price as well as the corresponding compensation as established by current regulations. If I do not receive a response within a reasonable time, I will be forced to escalate the complaint to the competent civil aviation authorities and resort to specialized claims platforms. I expect an urgent response and an immediate solution to this unfair situation. Sincerely, Maria

raw_text: Esperienza terribile, mai più. Esperienza terribile, da dimenticare!!! La compagnia non è stata in grado di fornire assistenza adeguata in svariate situazioni, causandoci grossi disagi e spese aggiuntive non indifferenti. Premetto che io e il mio compagno viaggiavamo con nostra figlia di 4 anni e con amici, anche loro con figli piccoli (il che, ovviamente , ha reso il disagio ancora maggiore). Ma nella nostra situazione si sono trovati altri passeggeri che facevano la nostra stessa tratta. Per cominciare, appena arrivati in Arabia Saudita, dal momento che avevano un doppio scalo (prima Jeddah e poi Riyadh), ci hanno obbligati ad acquistare un visto turistico, del costo di 90 euro a persona. Mai, né a Roma alla partenza né a Bangkok al ritorno, né tantomeno quando abbiamo acquistato il biglietto online (pagandolo meno proprio in virtù del doppio scalo) ci è stato spiegato chiaramente che, poiché prendevamo un volo nazionale in territorio saudita, ci occorreva un visto di transito o scalo, che è gratuito, anche se soggetto a tasse e assicurazioni, ma va richiesto con diversi giorni di anticipo (all'aeroporto di Jeddah ci hanno parlato di 5 giorni). Non avendolo fatto per tempo, ed essendo sempre stati rassicurati quando chiedevamo lumi a riguardo alla compagnia, all'aeroporto di Jeddah siamo stati costretti a comprare quello turistico, carissimo e con validità di un anno (il che è assurdo per chi deve semplicemente effettuare uno scalo). In tutto ciò, resta il dubbio che non solo non fosse necessario un visto turistico, ma forse nemmeno uno di transito, dal momento che sia sul sito della compagnia che su altri siti che danno info a riguardo (vedi Saudivisaoffice.com) è scritto che "con uno scalo da 12 a 96 ore è richiesto il visto di transito", mentre sotto le 12 ore parrebbe non servire nulla, ed era il nostro caso. Come se non bastasse, dopo questa bella sorpresa, che ha previsto un esborso di quasi 300 euro in più sul prezzo del biglietto, essendo noi una famiglia di 3 persone, il nostro volo per Riyadh viene ritardato di due ore e quindi ci arriva comunicazione via mail che il nostro volo è stato cancellato (perché ovviamente il ritardo ci avrebbe fatto perdere la coincidenza Riyadh-Roma) e quindi siamo stati spostati su un volo diretto Jeddah-Roma che parte la mattina dopo. Ormai sono le 21, noi siamo stanchi e arrabbiati e nessuno si occupa di noi. Dobbiamo insistere più volte per poter parlare con l'assistenza clienti Saudia e quando finalmente ci ricevono ci promettono di trovarci una sistemazione per la notte (che otteniamo a mezzanotte, quindi 3 ore dopo la comunicazione della cancellazione del nostro viaggio) e che probabilmente non avremmo mai ottenuto se non avessimo insistito. La mattina seguente finalmente partiamo per Roma su un volo della durata di oltre 5 ore che, ciliegina sulla torta, non ha nemmeno gli schermi (ed per giunta è in ritardo). Sorvolo sulla qualità del cibo perché ho già detto abbastanza.
translated_text: Terrible experience, never again. Terrible experience, to forget!!! The company was unable to provide adequate assistance in various situations, causing us great discomfort and significant additional expenses. I preface that my partner and I were traveling with our 4-year-old daughter and friends, also with small children (which obviously made the discomfort even greater). But in our situation, there were other passengers on the same route. To begin with, upon arrival in Saudi Arabia, since they had a double stopover (first Jeddah then Riyadh), we were forced to purchase a tourist visa costing 90 euros per person. Never, neither in Rome at departure nor in Bangkok on return, nor when we bought the ticket online (paying less precisely because of the double stopover) were we clearly told that since we were taking a domestic flight within Saudi territory, we needed a transit or stopover visa, which is free, although subject to taxes and insurance, but must be requested several days in advance (at Jeddah airport they told us 5 days). Not having done so in time, and always being reassured when asking the company about it, at Jeddah airport we were forced to buy the tourist visa, very expensive and valid for one year (which is absurd for someone just making a stopover). In all this, there remains the doubt that not only was a tourist visa unnecessary, but perhaps not even a transit visa, since both on the company website and other info sites (see Saudivisaoffice.com) it is written that "for a stopover from 12 to 96 hours a transit visa is required," while under 12 hours apparently nothing is needed, which was our case. As if that were not enough, after this unpleasant surprise, which meant an extra expense of almost 300 euros on the ticket price for our family of 3, our flight to Riyadh was delayed by two hours and we received an email that our flight was canceled (because obviously the delay would have made us miss the Riyadh-Rome connection) and so we were moved to a direct Jeddah-Rome flight departing the next morning. It was already 9 pm, we were tired and angry and no one took care of us. We had to insist several times to speak with Saudia customer service and when we finally got through they promised to find us accommodation for the night (which we got at midnight, 3 hours after the cancellation notice) and probably we would never have gotten it if we had not insisted. The next morning we finally left for Rome on a flight lasting over 5 hours which, to top it off, did not even have screens (and was delayed). I will skip commenting on the food quality because I have said enough.

raw_text:
Pessima esperienza, compagnia da evitare Prima e sicuramente ultima esperienza con Saudia: fatta eccezione per il bravissimo personale di volo e per due (DUE) addetti del personale di terra, tutto il resto è stato gestito in maniera tragicomica, tra indifferenza, assenza di informazioni, pochissima chiarezza, supponenza.
A partire sulle informazioni NON fornite sui 2 transfer in Arabia Saudita, Riyadh e Gedda, le cui tempistiche e modalità sono proposte proprio da Saudia, che però si è guardata bene dall'informare preventivamente circa la necessità di un VISTO.
Noi lo abbiamo scoperto per puro caso in aereoporto a Malpensa, poche ore prima della partenza. Esistono due VISTI, uno che costa una 20ina di Euro, lo Stopover Visa che si può fare online compilando un form, MA... si può fare una sola volta (informazione questa abbastanza fumosa, soprattutto se stai cercando di ottenerlo in fretta e furia con uno smartphone), l'altro è una E-Visa standard che ha validità un anno e che costa ben 90 euro... si può ottenere online? Si, compilando un form ai limiti dell'assurdo, ricordiamoci che tutto questo avviene in aereoporto e di fretta, visto che la compagnia non aveva fornito le dovute informazioni, e che il personale Saudia sa benissimo essere ai limiti della fattibilità di compilazione; l'alternativa è ottenerlo On Arrival, ovvero durante il transfer, con attese infinite e code chilometriche che guarda un po'... fanno perdere la coincidenza aerea. E' successo a molti (e attenzione, anche la coincidenza e le tempistiche, laddove a Riyadh bisogna addirittura uscire dall'aereoporto per cambiare terminal, le ha proposte proprio Saudia).
Noi abbiamo dovuto litigare per farci ricollocare su un altro volo, perchè a sentir loro avremmo dovuto pagare pure questo. Abbiamo atteso 3 ore davanti ad un desk dove nessuno ci diceva nulla, nè dava informazioni, sperando che la procedura andasse a buon fine.

Poi oltre il danno la beffa: il nostro bagaglio non arriverà mai alla destinazione finale (Giacarta). E qui comincia la follia dei rimbalzi tra aereoporti, in cui il bagaglio prima viene trovato e poi sparisce... ma resta "delayed" per ben 14 giorni. Solo al rientro ci scriveranno che il bagaglio è "lost" dunque avremo la possibilità di attivare le dovute procedure per i risarcimenti. Il bagaglio è ovviamente affidato a Saudia, in qualità di unico vettore. Ma con Saudia è impossibile parlare, si comunica unicamente attraverso mail alle quali non seguono risposte e loro messaggi precompilati ai quali non si può ribattere. La compilazione dell'ennesimo form impossibile per il bagaglio ci è costata TRE giorni di tentativi (form nel quale il valore degli oggetti è ancora espresso in Lire italiane, l'Euro per loro non esiste).
Inoltre, Saudia chiede a noi di chiamare il loro servizio clienti: un numero dell'Arabia Saudita, telefono fisso, per contattare il quale dovremmo spendere 2 € al minuto. Senza altre possibilità.

Ricapitolando: 1 volo perso, più di 100 € spesi per un visto di transito, 1 bagaglio perso. La procedura di rimborso (se mai ci sarà un rimborso), al di là del disagio durante il viaggio, è ancora in atto.
Se mai dovesse esserci qualche problema con Saudia sappiate che saranno solo cavoli vostri, non aspettatevi collaborazione, ma solo tanto tanto sangue amaro.
translated_text: Terrible experience, airline to avoid First and definitely last experience with Saudia: except for the very good flight staff and two (TWO) ground staff members, everything else was managed in a tragicomic way, with indifference, lack of information, very little clarity, arrogance. Starting with the information NOT provided about the 2 transfers in Saudi Arabia, Riyadh and Jeddah, whose timings and methods are proposed by Saudia itself, which however did not inform in advance about the need for a VISA. We discovered this by pure chance at Malpensa airport, a few hours before departure. There are two VISAS, one costing about 20 euros, the Stopover Visa which can be done online by filling out a form, BUT... it can only be done once (this information is quite unclear, especially if you are trying to get it quickly with a smartphone), the other is a standard E-Visa valid for one year and costing 90 euros... can it be obtained online? Yes, by filling out a form that is almost absurd, remember that all this happens at the airport and in a hurry, since the company did not provide the necessary information, and Saudia staff know very well that filling it out is almost impossible; the alternative is to get it On Arrival, during the transfer, with endless waits and long queues that, guess what... cause you to miss the connecting flight. This happened to many (and note, even the connection and timings, where in Riyadh you have to leave the airport to change terminals, were proposed by Saudia itself). We had to argue to be rebooked on another flight because according to them we would have had to pay for this too. We waited 3 hours in front of a desk where no one told us anything or gave information, hoping the procedure would succeed. Then, to add insult to injury: our luggage never arrived at the final destination (Jakarta). And here begins the madness of bouncing between airports, where the luggage is first found and then disappears... but remains "delayed" for 14 days. Only upon return did they write to us that the luggage is "lost" so we will have the possibility to activate the necessary procedures for compensation. The luggage is obviously entrusted to Saudia, as the sole carrier. But with Saudia it is impossible to talk, communication is only through emails that receive no replies and their pre-written messages to which you cannot respond. Filling out yet another impossible form for the luggage cost us THREE days of attempts (a form in which the value of the items is still expressed in Italian Lire, the Euro does not exist for them). Furthermore, Saudia asks us to call their customer service: a Saudi Arabia number, a landline, for which we would have to pay 2 € per minute. No other options. To summarize: 1 missed flight, more than 100 € spent for a transit visa, 1 lost luggage. The refund procedure (if there will ever be a refund), beyond the inconvenience during the trip, is still ongoing. If there should ever be any problem with Saudia, know that it will be your problem alone, do not expect cooperation, only a lot of bitter frustration.

raw_text:
✈️ One-Star Comedy Show in the Sky with Flynas ✈️ If you ever wanted to experience what it feels like to be both a sardine and a confused contestant on a reality TV show, Flynas is the airline for you.
• Check-in: It felt like I was auditioning for a role in Survivor: Airport Edition. Nobody knew what was happening, including the staff. By the time I got my boarding pass, I had aged five years.
• The Plane: I’ve seen bigger legroom in my kid’s car seat. Honestly, if you’re over 160 cm tall, prepare to fold yourself like origami.
• Food: They offered me something that I think was supposed to be a sandwich. It looked like it had survived three world wars and a heatwave.
• Service: The flight attendants smiled… but only the way people smile when they’re trying not to scream.
• Bonus entertainment: The turbulence gave us a free rollercoaster experience. 10/10 for thrills, 0/10 for relaxation.

Overall, Flynas is the perfect choice if you enjoy chaos, questionable snacks, and practicing advanced yoga poses in tiny seats. Otherwise, save yourself and book literally anything else.

⭐️ out of 5.

raw_text:
Deçue et dégoûtée Le retour de Jeddha fut sans doute le pire voyage en avion de ma vie et cela uniquement à cause du comportement du personnels à l’aéroport mais surtout dans l’avion.

En bref, dans l’aéroport au niveau des portique de e-douane maman a pas regarder la camera-écran en face et donc je lui explique comment cela fonctionne en français mais … en parallèle deux Saoudiens de la sécurité me crie littéralement dessus que je l’embrouille et de les laisser gérer. J’ai pas le temps d’écouter leur récrimination que la machine fonctionne et laisse passer maman. Tout est bien qui finit bien ? Et bien non les deux saoudiens se dirige vers nous bombant le torse et demandent en criant si on les avait pas entendu nous appeler. Misère.

Le second incident, plus grave, se déroule dans l’avion : Une saoudienne qui a une ranger pour elle toute seule (l’avion étant clairsemé de personnes) incline son siege fortement sur mon jeune frère, un jeune adolescent, qui lui entre deux personnes le supporte peu sur ce vol de 6h et lui demande gentiment si elle peut le remonter un peu … cet dernière appelle alors un steward qui crie littéralement en Arabe sur lui (sans même lui demander si le comprend) - meme pas la décence encore une fois d’utiliser le français ou l’anglais … et quand on lui fait remarquer il nous dir qu’il ont une hôtesse qui parle ukrainien (il se moque donc de nous). Un voyageur propose alors qu’on change soufiane dans un des nombreux siege vide - au vue du steward qui prend tribalement la defence de la saoudienne … ce dernier refuse expliquant qu’on a choisit délibérément nos place en ligne et que ces le droit de la voyageuse de nous écraser avec son siege.

Vous comprendrez aisément que ces deux points on pris le dessus sur le reste … en discutant un peu avec d’autres voyageurs : leur avis reste très négatif sur l’aéroport ou la compagnie. C’est vraiment dommage car il ne manque pas grand chose pour améliorer cela et permettre à plus de monde de découvrir Jeddha, en tant que destination de voyage ou d’escale.
translated_text: Disappointed and disgusted. The return from Jeddah was probably the worst plane trip of my life, solely due to the behavior of the staff at the airport and especially on the plane. Briefly, at the e-customs gate, my mother did not look at the camera screen, so I explained how it works in French, but two Saudi security guards literally shouted at me to stop confusing them and to let them handle it. Before I could listen to their complaints, the machine worked and let my mother pass. All well that ends well? No, the two Saudis approached us, puffed up their chests, and shouted if we hadn't heard them calling us. Miserable. The second, more serious incident happened on the plane: a Saudi woman had a row to herself (the plane was sparsely occupied) and reclined her seat strongly onto my younger brother, a young teenager, who was between two people and barely tolerated it on this 6-hour flight. He politely asked if she could raise it a bit. She then called a steward who literally shouted at him in Arabic (without even asking if he understood) – not even the decency to use French or English. When we pointed this out, he said they had a hostess who spoke Ukrainian (mocking us). A passenger suggested moving Soufiane to one of the many empty seats, but the steward, taking tribal defense of the Saudi woman, refused, explaining that we deliberately chose our seats in a row and that it was the traveler's right to crush us with her seat. You can easily understand that these two points overshadowed everything else. Talking with other travelers, their opinion of the airport and airline remains very negative. It's really a shame because it wouldn't take much to improve and allow more people to discover Jeddah as a travel or stopover destination.

raw_text:
Worst business class service experienced. Saudia needs to do better. As this is the National holiday week in Singapore, I thought of giving Saudia a try since they are offering the cheapest fare.

Crew service is appalling for Business class. Crew service from AirAsia and Scoot could have fared so much better. But I am utterly disappointed.

Firstly they did not know that the airline they work in are receiving guests from Singapore to fly to Bali. They don't know the staff are boarding from Singapore. They look puzzled and asked me if I onboard from Jeddah. I explained that I onboard from Singapore and not Jeddah, and they were talking amongst themselves that they didn't show about this new process.

Food presentation is good with good variety but for business class, crew doesn't bother to explain the dinner menu to me.

Crew serving business class is also very noisy, chit-chatting and gossiping loudly at the galley. And they even attempt to open my door despite I was asking them to wait while I was inside the lavatory without any apologies given.

Onboard entertainment is okay however there is no WiFi.

Business seat configuration is good with adequate privacy and comfortable.

Overall it is a totally appalling and disappointed experience.

raw_text:
Saudi Airlines worst service support at JFK I am writing to express my deep disappointment with the customer service provided by Saudi Airlines at JFK Airport in New York.

There was a minor discrepancy between the name on my booking and the name on my passport. Despite this, I was allowed to travel from Bahrain to New York without any issue. However, when I attempted to return to Bahrain, the airline staff at JFK refused to issue my boarding pass due to the same name mismatch.

The Saudi Airlines staff at JFK were uncooperative and unhelpful. I was provided with a customer service number, (800) 472-8342, which operates from 9:00 AM. Unfortunately, my flight (SV 20) had a gate closing time of 10:00 AM. During this short window, I made multiple calls and spoke with different agents, but most calls were either dropped midway or disconnected early. Each time, I had to repeat all my booking details from the beginning, with no progress or resolution. One agent kept me on the line for nearly 20 minutes but ultimately failed to assist. Around 10:00 AM, the check-in counter was closed, and I was left with no choice but to purchase a new one-way ticket for $1,157.86.

Upon returning to Bahrain, I contacted the local Saudi Airlines help desk at +973 1721 1550 to file a complaint. I was shocked to learn that the name on the ticket could have been corrected at the airport in just 2–3 minutes by paying a fee of only 45 SAR.

To add to the frustration, when I later attempted to request a refund for the unused ticket, I was informed that a no-show fee would be applied — despite the fact that the issue arose due to the airline's failure to provide timely assistance.

I sincerely hope Saudi Airlines will review this matter seriously and take the necessary steps to resolve my complaint fairly.

raw_text:
Saudia Airlines – Worst Airline Ever! No Customer Service & Zero Accountability I had the absolute worst experience with Saudia Airlines, and I feel obligated to warn others—DO NOT FLY WITH THEM!

Our problems started with a delay on our flight from Amsterdam, which caused us to miss our connecting flight in Jeddah. We had paid extra for a shorter layover, yet we were forced to wait an additional 4 hours at the airport with no assistance or compensation.

Then, when we finally arrived in Dubai, both my luggage and my travel companion’s luggage were missing. My suitcase was returned after three days, but my companion’s luggage was never found. This ruined our business trip, forcing us to spend hundreds of euros on new clothes and essentials.

Trying to get in touch with Saudia Airlines has been a nightmare. We have sent multiple emails, Instagram messages, and official complaints—ZERO RESPONSE. Even when we personally went to their office at the airport on our way back, the staff was shockingly rude, refusing to help and telling us it was “our own problem.”

I have now taken formal action by sending a registered letter demanding compensation, but once again, no response. This review is my final warning—if they continue to ignore my legal request, I will take them to court.

This airline is completely unprofessional, irresponsible, and dishonest. They take your money, provide horrible service, and refuse to take any responsibility. If you value your time, money, and sanity, stay far away from Saudia Airlines!

raw_text:
Newer fly Flynas! Why I STRICTLY don't recommend to use Flynas?

I had a flight from Istanbul (SAW) to Mumbai (BOM) at February, 28, 2023.
When I arrived to Mumbai I found out that my luggage was absent. I wrote the claim about the lost luggage, received PIR document and the personnel, who was serving the "lost luggage counter" told me that I will be informed about my luggage in a few days.
In the luggage I had all neccessary equipment for the mountain trekking, all my vital belongings. And I put a focus on this while communicating with the staff of the lost things service.
I’ve been waiting for three weeks but neither Flynas nor anyone in their authority including customer support has responded.
Numerous times during this period I wrote to "customer help chat" and asked them to provide me the information about my luggage but every time I received the standard message "as soon as possible" or just get ignored after my question.
Also I contacted Flynas CSU department and received nothing - they just ignored me.
I tryed to contact with flynas via facebook but it was the same story.
Finally after more than one year of ignorence and resending me from one department to another I received the response from Flynas that they are ready to pay me the refund. In order to receive the payment I had to provide them a copy of my passport and the bank account details what I did of course. But later on I received another response from Flynas that my bank account details are wrong and my case closed.
Again I started the procedure I connected with Flynas departments via their website customer service chat and other official contacts. And the old story repeated: they passed me from one department to another, ignored my questions, sent me letters with the messages like"wrong bank account" without any reasonable explanations, offers to wait only God knows how long: "as soon as possible", ignorence d so on.
I have a bulk of the screenshots as the evidence how they behave with the customers.
IMHO Flynas just was wasting my time.
Two years passed but still my case is not solved!
I believe that Flynas deliberately engaged in such activities in order to avoid fulfilling its obligations to the client.

raw_text: The worst airline ever! Lost baggage and ZERO RESPONSE! I travelled with Saudia for the first time. While returning to Amsterdam, they lost my main luggage which was supposedly not on boarded at the layover in Jeddah. It's been 20 days already, trying to find the missing luggage and there is just NO RESPONSE from them yet. We have tried everything, but they just do not seem to care or respond to any claim reports. On top of it, their flight stewards were extremely rude and looked as if they were forced to work. The staff at their security check was even ruder and insensitive. They threw our cabin luggage in the garbage and no one there knew English. After 30-40 minutes of turmoil and struggle, we could have it back. But the luggage is still missing for 20 days with absolutely no trace. It had belongings of emotional value that money will never be able to buy. I still pray and wish it comes back. I would never recommend this airline to anyone and would never certainly fly again.

Я отдал им свой внутренний паспорт, от него естественно ничего не поняли и попросил у меня фотографию на моем мобильном телефоне подтверждающую, что Я был в России. ладно если эти глупые вопросы задавали пограничники Я бы ничего не сказал но это не их дела!!! только нервы поднимали
translated_text: Horrible. Unfortunately, this is a disrespectful company. Here’s what happened: I have a Russian passport with stamps from twenty countries, but since I was born in Syria, they started an investigation as if they were border guards and demanded a document proving that I am Russian. I gave them my internal passport, which they obviously did not understand, and they asked me for a photo on my mobile phone proving that I had been in Russia. I showed them my photo next to Red Square, and only then did they give me a boarding pass. Honestly, it's the first time I've seen such stupidity. My passport has stamps proving that I have flown before. If these stupid questions were asked by border guards, I wouldn't have said anything, but it's none of their business!!! They just raised my nerves.

raw_text: Nearly suffocated On the first journey from London to Jeddah, there were no problems and everything went smoothly. However, on the return flight, after we boarded the plane, it was found that the air-conditioning wasn't working. Incredibly, we were held for about two hours in temperatures of about 35C. It felt like we were going to suffocate. It was a genuinely scary experience because we were trapped in the plane with little to no communication from the captain. They should have taken us back to the gate and let us out while they solved the problem. Anyway, I made an official complaint through their website but still, after three weeks, haven't received any kind of reply of apology from them. So my advice is to avoid this airline. They are clearly a cheap budget operation and don't live up to the standards one would expect from the Saudi flag-carrier. That said the flight itself was comfortable enough and the food was good. But don't expect any customer service if anything goes wrong. "You pays your money and you takes your chances!"

raw_text:
They Left Us Stranded, Cancelled Our Flights Without Warning – The Worst Airline Experience of My Life I have never in my life written a review like this, but Saudia Airlines left us heartbroken, helpless, and financially wrecked. Please read this if you're considering booking with them—you need to know how badly they treat their passengers.

We booked flights from London to Riyadh, then connecting to Jeddah, then Nairobi. At NO point during the booking process were we informed that we needed a transit visa to pass through Jeddah. We only found out on the day of our flight, while already at the airport.

We were told we could get the visa on arrival in Riyadh—but when we landed and explained to the visa counter that we had only a 2-hour stopover, they deliberately delayed us. I’m not exaggerating—they were eating, drinking tea, joking around, and pretending to be confused. It took over 1.5 hours for them to issue a simple visa, while we stood there pleading. As a result, we missed our connecting flight.

No one helped us. No one cared. We were stranded in a foreign country through NO fault of our own. Saudia staff shrugged and left us to figure it out. We had to book expensive last-minute tickets on Qatar Airways just to reach Nairobi.

But it gets worse.

When we finally made it to Nairobi, we found out that Saudia had canceled our return flights. No explanation. No email. No notification. NOTHING. Just… gone. We paid for 6 flights and only used ONE—and they refused to help or refund anything.

Customer service has been stone cold, robotic, and unhelpful. No one has taken responsibility. No apology. No accountability.

Saudia Airlines treated us like we didn’t matter. I’ve never felt so dismissed and disrespected by a company in my life.

DO NOT BOOK WITH SAUDIA. I wouldn’t wish this experience on anyone.

Flyadeal Closed the Gate Early and Left Us Behind – Then Blamed Us As a frequent traveler for over 40 years, I never thought I’d experience something like this.

On April 21, 2025, my daughter and I were booked on Flyadeal flight F3 761. We arrived at the airport early, passed security, and were waiting in the gate area. However, the boarding pass had no gate number, and no proper announcements or final call were made.

Later, we learned that boarding began 45 minutes before departure and lasted only about 15 minutes. We arrived at the gate 28 minutes before the scheduled departure, yet we were still denied boarding. The ground staff simply told us it was too late — even though the plane was still on the ground.

We were shocked. We had to purchase expensive last-minute tickets with another airline just to get back home to Cairo, which caused significant financial and emotional stress.

The worst part? Flyadeal later emailed us falsely claiming we had “already boarded.” This is completely untrue and deeply disrespectful.

No refund. No apology. No accountability.

If you value professionalism, communication, or basic decency, I strongly urge you to avoid Flyadeal.

Don't fly with Saudi Airlines. Avoid any under cost to save your holidays. Hi There,

I complaint to Saudi Airlines and they never responded. I tried contacting them several times.

I am a frequent flyer. I never experienced such a horrible experience ever in my life. I bought four tickets from London-Riyadh-Jeddah. Four of us flew in Flight number SV0122. Madina to Dhaka(SV0798) our flight was 1900(26th October). Flight time changed to 0300 am(27th Oct). I reached the airport at 1500(26th oct). I went to checked in to drop our luggage. No one informed us anything until 1700 pm when I noticed my boarding pass. I went to the Saudi airline office inside the airport. They said flight time changed 7 days earlier. They said this is not their fault that we don’t know. But there wasn’t any evidence that Saudi Airlines informed us. officers asked me to complain to Saudi Airlines. I even asked them for some facility’s (I.e food, drinks and some space to take rest but they refused everything). My mum is old and not well. We all had to sit in the airport chair over 13 hours. Then I noticed flight became delayed more and we ended up to fly at 1645(27th oct). My mum became ill. After doing Umrah and sitting in the airport over 13 hours in the cold we all became unwell(1st part).

From Dhaka to London- I got a notification from my travel agency(still no update from airlines) that my return flight was rescheduled and it moved four days forward. I called several times to Dhaka office but no one could help me. I had to go physically. When I reached there three people were missing from their chair. Only one person in the office. When I asked the security man regarding rest of the staff he replied everyone went to toilet break. I waited 15 minutes and one person came back and told me that she can’t do anything regarding the flight. I had to wait another 15 minutes to see the Manager(Mr Zahid) who helped me to changed the flight from 14th of November to 10th of November. I informed the manager that why there is no communication from airlines. He checked and assured that from now on there will update from airline directly to me. From 1st of November to 10th of November there were 7 times flight time and date changed but I didn’t receive any communication from airline. I received few emails to update my seat to business class. Every time I received the update from other people. Last update was my flight was at 10th November at 12:30 am. I was on my way to airport and got an update from other agencies that my flight is cancelled and rescheduled after 5 days. I called Manager(Mr Zahid) and he said he doesn’t know anything about the cancellation of flight. He suggested me to go to the airport and ask for help. When I reached the airport there wasn’t anyone in the office. Lots of people were waiting in front of the office. Later one person came and started to help. He was alone in the office and lots of people were shouting. He asked for help but no one came to help him. After few hours of waiting he managed to give me flight instead of 1230 am to 0730 am(Bangladesh biman). They also provided me a hotel to take rest for few hours but hotel was in an extremely poor condition. After this experience I believe I need a compensation.

Reservation code- DLXERY

Airline Reservation code- TFPGW7

Airlines-Saudia SV0798

E ticket- 0655830471591u

Would you mind to send this email to a relevant person who has some time to help me out. As a British citizen I treated badly.
Kind regards
Barrister Zahid Azad
Email- zhdazad@yahoo.co.uk

raw_text:
Worst airline ever We had a very disappointing and unprofessional experience that occurred today (3 November 2025) during check-in for my Flyadeal flight from Riyadh (Terminal 4) to Dubai, traveling with my wife and young child.
At the check-in counter, your staff refused to accept my child’s carseat, claiming it needed to be in a box or sealed. The carseat was properly packed inside a dedicated flight travel cover specifically designed for air travel, which fully meets airline standards.

The check-in agent was slow, uncooperative, and appeared to be under training, with another staff member beside her instructing her. That trainer incorrectly told her that the carseat “needs to be sealed.” When I asked whether it could be checked in as it was, the agent said “no.”
I then went to the manager for assistance. He initially said he couldn’t help, repeating the same incorrect information. When I politely asked him for two minutes of his time, he replied, “I don’t have two minutes for you,” and refused to assist further.

Because of this, I was forced to throw away my child’s carseat, which cost SAR 420, in order to check in and board the flight. This was an extremely distressing and frustrating experience for my family, especially as we were traveling with a family and small child.

raw_text:
Un’esperienza disastrosa La nostra esperienza con Saudia Airlines è stata semplicemente disastrosa. Il volo è stato posticipato più volte e, dopo essere stati imbarcati e aver visto l’aereo muoversi, siamo stati fatti scendere senza alcuna spiegazione. Abbiamo aspettato ore in aeroporto di notte, per poi essere trasferiti in hotel senza alcuna assistenza adeguata.

Da due giorni siamo bloccati, senza informazioni chiare. Ci hanno addirittura cancellato un altro volo e siamo stati costretti a gestire tutto da soli. La compagnia ci aveva detto di restare svegli di notte per attendere una mail con i nuovi biglietti aerei, che però non è mai arrivata. Alla fine, siamo stati noi a dover insistere per ottenere un nuovo volo e per recuperare i bagagli.

Nel frattempo, siamo rimasti senza vestiti, medicine e beni essenziali, poiché i bagagli erano trattenuti dalla compagnia. La situazione è stata gestita in modo totalmente inefficiente e irrispettoso, causandoci la perdita di giorni di lavoro e un livello di stress mentale ed emotivo insostenibile.

Un’esperienza pessima, gestita nel peggior modo possibile. Saudia Airlines si è dimostrata inaffidabile e incapace di gestire le emergenze. Non voleremo mai più con questa compagnia e la sconsigliamo vivamente a chiunque.
translated_text: A disastrous experience. Our experience with Saudia Airlines was simply disastrous. The flight was delayed multiple times and, after being boarded and seeing the plane move, we were made to disembark without any explanation. We waited for hours at the airport at night, only to be transferred to a hotel without any adequate assistance. We have been stuck for two days, without clear information. They even canceled another flight and we were forced to manage everything ourselves. The airline told us to stay awake at night to wait for an email with the new flight tickets, which never arrived. In the end, we had to insist on getting a new flight and retrieving our luggage. In the meantime, we were left without clothes, medicine, and essential goods, as the luggage was held by the airline. The situation was handled in a completely inefficient and disrespectful manner, causing us to lose days of work and an unsustainable level of mental and emotional stress. A terrible experience, managed in the worst possible way. Saudia Airlines has proven to be unreliable and incapable of handling emergencies. We will never fly with this airline again and strongly advise against it to anyone.

Compagnie bonne pour transporter des animaux pas des gens ! Horrible, à fuir absolument !!!! Si la plupart des gens se plaignent des retards de vols de cette compagnie, nous concernant à l'aller comme au retour l'avion était à l'heure. Les problèmes se situent ailleurs.

Nous n'avons pas choisi la compagnie aérienne elle nous a été imposée par l'agence. Si on avait su ... on aurait carrément changé d'agence

Cette compagnie est fait pour transporter des ANIMAUX et non des personnes

1. Un accueil catastrophique, aimables comme une porte de prison TOUS les interlocuteurs flynas auxquels nous avons eu à faire nous ont traité comme du bétail.
2. La violence verbale et même physique des stewarts/hotesses. Les hotesses se fichent que vous ayez sonné ou non, et qd elles daignent venir vous voir et que vous demandez à acheter un café elle ne revient plus du tout. Le stewart à l'aller le 24/12 vol MARSEILLE - JEDDAH : FLYNAS 660, a carrément poussé violemment un homme qui était sur son passage et qui peinait à ranger son sac, le type a atterri assis sur le siège il n'a rien compris.
3. Les mensonges à répétition. Ou le zero info. Au retour, DJEDDAH MARSEILLE FLYNAS 659, nous sommes 5 personnes à avoir demandé un surclassement payant. OUI il y a de la place, nous avons payé chacun le supplément. Au moment du dépose bagage on demande où se situe le lounge, et le gars nous indique carrément et sans aucune politesse l'endroit. En y arrivant, on nous interdit d'entrer car FLYNAS n'a pas de lounge (la compagnie est trop mer...que pour se payer ça).
Arrivés dans l'avion, on nous indique nos places à l'avant de l'avion. Le stewart nous dit que NON vous n'etes pas en 1ère. Pourtant nous avons payé pour 5 personnes.
Me voyant en colère il propose de réparer ce geste en m'offrant ... un café ou une petite bouteille d'eau !!!!
5. La mauvaise organisation
6. La saleté
7. Le manque d'accessoires etc : ATTENTION chargez vos téléphones il ny a aucune prise ou prise USB pour recharger quoi que ce soit
8. TOUT est payant . Et meme qd vous voulez payer ils nont plus rien !!!

Bref, vous l'aurez compris, on se demande comment ils peuvent encore transporter des gens. PLUS JAMAIS on ne fera appel à cette bande d'escrocs.

Payez vous une vraie compagnie mettez le prix, car nous ne sommes pas des animaux.
translated_text: A good company for transporting animals, not people! Horrible, absolutely to be avoided!!!! While most people complain about flight delays with this company, for us, both going and returning, the plane was on time. The problems lie elsewhere. We did not choose the airline; it was imposed on us by the agency. If we had known... we would have completely changed agencies. This company is meant to transport ANIMALS and not people. 1. A catastrophic welcome, as friendly as a prison door. ALL the flynas representatives we dealt with treated us like cattle. 2. The verbal and even physical violence of the stewards/hostesses. The hostesses don't care if you rang the bell or not, and when they deign to come to you and you ask to buy a coffee, they never come back. The steward on the flight on 24/12 from MARSEILLE - JEDDAH: FLYNAS 660, violently pushed a man who was in his way and struggling to stow his bag; the guy landed sitting in his seat, completely confused. 3. Repeated lies. Or zero information. On the return, DJEDDAH MARSEILLE FLYNAS 659, five of us asked for a paid upgrade. YES, there is space, we each paid the supplement. At the baggage drop, we asked where the lounge was, and the guy pointed us to the place without any politeness. Upon arrival, we were forbidden to enter because FLYNAS has no lounge (the company is too cheap to afford that). Arriving on the plane, we were shown our seats at the front of the plane. The steward told us NO, you are not in first class. Yet we paid for 5 people. Seeing me angry, he offered to make up for this gesture by offering me... a coffee or a small bottle of water!!!! 5. Poor organization. 6. Dirtiness. 7. Lack of accessories, etc.: BE CAREFUL, charge your phones; there are no plugs or USB ports to recharge anything. 8. EVERYTHING is paid. And even when you want to pay, they have nothing left!!! In short, you understand, we wonder how they can still transport people. NEVER AGAIN will we use this gang of crooks. Pay for a real airline, pay the price, because we are not animals.

Unfair policy I booked a family holiday directly through the Saudi Airlines website and paid extra for extra legroom seats for the whole journey. A couple of months later, Saudi Airlines changed my flights due to operational reasons.

When I logged a complaint about the extra legroom seats I’d already paid for, I was told that the fee was non-refundable and non-transferable. If I wanted extra legroom seats on the new flights, I’d have to pay for them again.

It feels unfair to lose money I already spent on something I didn’t even get to use. I would have expected a more reasonable solution, especially when booking directly with the airline.

raw_text:
Excellent Flights & Crew This is the third time flying long-haul with Saudi Airlines which did not disappoint.
Check-in at Manchester was a smooth pleasant experience with no issues.
Whilst boarding was chaotic this was mainly due to the fault of airport staff and not the airline. The operatives attending to wheelchair passengers assuming a self important role and disregarding the comfort and safety of other passengers.
Once on board however I was warmly greeted by the cabin crew and shown to my seat. A choice of drinks soon followed accompanied by Arabic coffee and dates.
The seating arrangement on the 787 aircraft is a 1-2-1 arrangement providing an intimate and calm environment for a maximum of 24 passengers.
Shortly after take off I was presented with a nice amenity kit and menu with the chef talking me through the extensive food and drink options available. I subsequently ordered lunch and picked a time for service.
The attentive cabin crew duly laid the table with three well presented and delicious courses with excellent drinks.
I then settled back on the fully reclining seating arrangement being entertained throughout by the impressive music, movie and TV entertainment system .
The flight was a memorable and pleasant expense well worth the highly competitive fare.
Upon my arrival in Saudi Arabia I enjoyed a three hour layover within the Saudia (complimentary) business lounge followed by a very similar flight and experience on the connecting flight to Malaysia.
I travel again to Asia in December and had no hesitation whatsoever on booking my flight with Saudia Airlines.

Do not buy anything with this company, really, that is my recommendation.

raw_text: Angenehme Reise mit Saudia Ich war zuerst skeptisch, da es viele schlechte Bewertungen über Saudia gibt aber ich denke, dass die meisten Menschen eher eine Rezension schreiben, wenn was nicht gepasst hat, statt wenn alles gut lief. Daher dachte ich mir, ich schreibe nach dem Hinflug mal eine Rezension. Wir sind zuerst von Zürich nach Riad mit einem A320 geflogen. Die Sitze waren sehr bequem, es gab eine gute Beinfreiheit und der Service war sehr gut. Auch das Essen hat sehr gut geschmeckt. Wie haben in einem kleinen Beutel eine Schlafmaske, ein paar Socken und Ohropax sowie eine Zahnbürste und eine kleine Tube Zahnpasta bekommen. Getränke wurden immer wieder serviert. Der Umstieg in Riad hat problemlos geklappt. Von Riad nach Bangkok ging es dann mit einer 787-9 (Dteamliner) weiter. Es war wieder ein sehr angenehmer Flug. Der Service war wiede sehr gut, obwohl es ein Nachtflug war. Uns wurde ein warmes Essen gut eine Stunde nach Abflug serviert und kurz vor der Landung gab es dann noch ein Stück Pizza. Beides war geschmacklich sehr gut. Ich bin die gleiche Strecke schon mit Etihad und auch mehrmals mit Thai geflogen. Ich finde es gibt keinen großen Unterschied. Die Sitze bei Saudia sind definitiv viel bequemer wie bei den anderen Fluglinien, dafür ist vielleicht das Entertainment Programm bei den Anderen umfangreicher. Aber mit 5 deutschsprachigen aktuellen Filmen und weiteren 5-6 in Englisch fand ich es definitiv ausreichend! Und das Preis -Leistungs Verhältnis ist wirklich unschlagbar.
translated_text: Pleasant trip with Saudia I was initially skeptical because there are many bad reviews about Saudia, but I think most people write a review when something goes wrong rather than when everything goes well. So I thought I'd write a review after the outbound flight. We first flew from Zurich to Riyadh on an A320. The seats were very comfortable, there was good legroom, and the service was very good. The food also tasted very good. We received a small bag with a sleep mask, some socks, earplugs, a toothbrush, and a small tube of toothpaste. Drinks were served repeatedly. The transfer in Riyadh went smoothly. From Riyadh to Bangkok, we continued with a 787-9 (Dreamliner). It was again a very pleasant flight. The service was very good, even though it was a night flight. We were served a hot meal about an hour after takeoff and shortly before landing there was a piece of pizza. Both tasted very good. I have flown the same route with Etihad and several times with Thai. I find there is no big difference. The seats on Saudia are definitely much more comfortable than on other airlines, but perhaps the entertainment program on the others is more extensive. But with 5 German-language current movies and another 5-6 in English, I found it definitely sufficient! And the price-performance ratio is really unbeatable.

raw_text:
Slecht Maatschappij!!!! Zeer slechte ervaring met Saudia Airlines – vlucht geannuleerd en geen enkele service of compensatie

Op 12 juli zouden wij vertrekken van Amsterdam naar Manila met Saudia Airlines. Wat volgde was een nachtmerrie van slechte communicatie, onduidelijkheid, en complete desinteresse in de passagier.

Onze vlucht stond gepland om 16:00 uur, maar door technische problemen moesten we het vliegtuig verlaten. We kregen een voucher voor diner en mochten later opnieuw instappen. Toen het vliegtuig een stukje achteruit reed, stopte het weer. De kapitein meldde dat er opnieuw een technisch probleem was en dat we terug naar de gate moesten. Uiteindelijk, rond middernacht, kregen we te horen dat de vlucht was geannuleerd.

Er werd totaal geen opvang geregeld: geen hotel, geen vervoer, geen duidelijke communicatie. We moesten zelf terug naar huis en kregen alleen een standaard e-mail van Saudia. Er was geen compensatie, geen hulp, niets. Alsof dat nog niet genoeg was, bleek ook mijn koffer beschadigd door deze vlucht.

Sinds juli probeer ik contact op te nemen met Saudia Airlines, maar zonder enig resultaat. Er is geen werkend klantenservice-e-mailadres, de telefoonnummers op de website werken niet of zijn onbruikbaar, en de medewerkers die ik aan de lijn kreeg spraken gebrekkig Engels en konden niets betekenen. Het uploaden van documenten via hun website is vrijwel onmogelijk door technische beperkingen.

Nu, drie maanden later, heb ik nog steeds geen enkele vorm van compensatie ontvangen en is mijn klacht volledig genegeerd. Dit is absoluut onacceptabel.

Ik raad Saudia Airlines ten zeerste af. De service is waardeloos en als er iets misgaat, laten ze je volledig in de steek.
translated_text:
Bad company!!!! Very bad experience with Saudia Airlines – flight canceled and no service or compensation.

On July 12, we were supposed to depart from Amsterdam to Manila with Saudia Airlines. What followed was a nightmare of poor communication, confusion, and complete disregard for the passenger.

Our flight was scheduled for 16:00, but due to technical problems, we had to leave the plane. We received a voucher for dinner and were allowed to board again later. When the plane reversed a bit, it stopped again. The captain announced another technical problem and that we had to return to the gate. Finally, around midnight, we were told the flight was canceled.

No accommodation was arranged: no hotel, no transport, no clear communication. We had to go home ourselves and only received a standard email from Saudia. There was no compensation, no help, nothing. As if that wasn't enough, my suitcase was also damaged during this flight.

Since July, I have tried to contact Saudia Airlines but without any result. There is no working customer service email, the phone numbers on the website do not work or are unusable, and the staff I spoke to had poor English and could not help. Uploading documents via their website is almost impossible due to technical limitations.

Now, three months later, I still have not received any compensation and my complaint has been completely ignored. This is absolutely unacceptable.

I strongly advise against Saudia Airlines. The service is worthless and if something goes wrong, they leave you completely stranded.

raw_text: De slechtste vluchtervaring die ik ooit heb gehad De eerste ervaring was door vertraging op de eerste dag, zoals niemand ons vertelde vanwege de vertraging, en toen werd de vlucht geannuleerd. Ja, ze compenseerden ons voor het annuleren van het slapen in het hotel tot de tweede dag, omdat we gepland waren om met een alternatief vliegtuig te verhuizen. Op deze dag was het vliegtuig 5 uur vertraagd om op te stijgen zonder dat iemand ons de reden vertelde. Ik probeerde het bedrijf te bellen, maar ik vond geen antwoord!!! raw_text:
Rude Treatment on SV803: Saudia’s Service Depends on Where You Come From Flight Review: Saudia SV803 (Dhaka → Jeddah) & SV215 (Jeddah → Amsterdam)
Date of Travel: 18 August 2025
Route: Dhaka (DAC) → Jeddah (JED) → Amsterdam (AMS)
Airline: Saudia (Saudi Arabian Airlines)

Segment 1: Dhaka to Jeddah (SV803)
Unfortunately, this part of the journey left me with a very unpleasant impression. It was my first international flight, and I was genuinely excited and nervous at the same time. Sadly, that excitement was quickly replaced by discomfort due to the behavior of the cabin crew on this segment.
From the start, the flight attendants’ demeanor felt cold and unwelcoming. It seemed as though our presence was somehow inconvenient to them. The most disappointing moment came when I politely asked a flight attendant for a glass of water. Her response was:
“We don’t have water at the moment; you have to wait.”
That statement might sound normal on paper, but it was the tone that was deeply hurtful — sharp, scolding, and dismissive. She could have easily said it in a neutral or kind manner, but instead, it felt like she was reprimanding me for asking. It made me feel small, especially as a first-time international traveler.
I didn’t say anything back, but the interaction stayed with me for the entire flight. It created an uncomfortable atmosphere that overshadowed the rest of the journey. I noticed that other passengers from Bangladesh were treated in a similarly indifferent way — almost as if our requests were bothersome.

Segment 2: Jeddah to Amsterdam (SV215) - Saudia
On this leg, the tone shifted completely. The flight attendants were polite and professional. It almost felt like a different airline altogether.
This sharp contrast made me question why there was such a difference in attitude between the two flights but similar airline. When I shared this story with friends and relatives later, many said they had experienced the same inconsistency — the level of respect and warmth seemed to depend on where passengers were coming from, where they were headed, or even how they looked.

Overall Impression
This was supposed to be a special milestone for me as it was my first journey beyond my country’s borders. Instead, Saudia left a bitter first impression. The airline’s conduct on the first leg of the journey felt dismissive and discriminatory, which is the last thing anyone should experience while flying. Air travel should make passengers feel safe, comfortable, and respected, not judged or unwanted.

Most incompetent airline service I have ever experienced. booked a ticket from Casablanca to Tashkent with a transit in Jeddah. My experience with this airline was an absolute nightmare.

The first flight from Casablanca was delayed by over 4 hours. Instead of accommodating me properly, I was denied boarding because they claimed I wouldn’t make my connection in Jeddah. I was rebooked for the next day and promised hotel accommodation and meal vouchers. I never received any meal vouchers, and the hotel was filthy — dirty room, used bed sheets, and completely unacceptable conditions.

The next day, things got even worse. Despite holding valid boarding passes for the rebooked flight, I was denied boarding again in Casablanca, this time because I was “not in the system.” No real assistance was offered, and I was left stranded for the second day in a row.

This airline failed on every level: delays, incompetence, lack of communication, zero accountability, and no respect for passengers. Instead of being treated with basic care, I was ignored and dismissed.

If you value your time, money, or sanity, avoid this airline, especially if you have to travel from a Moroccan airport. Their personal is just on another level of incompetence.

Worst Nightmare If I could give zero stars to Saudia Airlines, I would. My experience was nothing short of a nightmare.

I checked in two bags on my flight from New York (JFK) to Jeddah (May 14, 2025). Due to unforeseen circumstances, I couldn’t complete my journey — but my luggage did. Since then, Saudia has completely failed to assist me in recovering it. Despite multiple emails with all details provided (names, flight number, baggage tags, photos, etc.), their baggage services team has shown nothing but silence and negligence.

No claim number, no updates, no effort to locate the bags — just endless frustration. These bags contain essential personal items, and Saudia’s inaction is unacceptable for an international airline that claims to care about its passengers.

Saudia clearly does not value customer service or responsibility. Losing baggage happens — but refusing to respond, assist, or take ownership is unprofessional and disgraceful. I am now forced to escalate this issue through aviation authorities, consumer protection, and legal action.

Travelers beware: if Saudia mishandles your baggage or fails to deliver on basic service, don’t expect any accountability. This airline has been my worst travel experience to date

raw_text: Aus der Verantwortung stehlen! Diese Airline stiehlt sich aus ihrer Verantwortung. Während mein Gepäck unter der Verantwortung der Airline war, wurde mein Koffer beschädigt. Dies habe ich umgehend bei meiner Ankunft dokumentiert. Wir reden hier vom 06.NOV2024 und ich schreibe diese Zeilen am 09. März 2025: 5 Monate nach dem Geschehen!! - Die Kommunikation mit FLYNAS ist schwierig: eMails werden gar nicht oder nur sehr schleppend beantwortet. Man ist auf teure Telefonate angewiesen, die in meinem Fall nur nutzlose Versprechen beinhalteten. Bis heute (5 Monate) wurde der entstandene Kofferschaden nicht reguliert!! Airlines haben eigentlich für solche Fälle eine Versicherung. Andere Fluggesellschaften regulieren viel schneller. Ich habe das Gefühl FLYNAS will sich aus der Verantwortung stehlen, und zieht deshalb das Verfahren in die Länge - in der Hoffnung, das man als Geschädigter aufgibt. Wenn es schon bei solch einer Kleinigkeit wie einen Kofferschaden zu solchen Problemen kommt, dann möchte ich nicht wissen/erleben wie man sich bei einer extremen Verspätung, Flugausfall oder ähnlichem verhält. Für mich ist eins sicher - das war mein erster und letzter Flug mit FLYNAS. Wenn Sie bei auftretenden Problem nicht ohne Hilfe dastehen möchten - dann kann ich nur empfehlen FLYNAS zu meiden.
translated_text: This airline shirks its responsibility! While my luggage was under the airline's responsibility, my suitcase was damaged. I documented this immediately upon my arrival. We are talking about November 6, 2024, and I am writing these lines on March 9, 2025: 5 months after the incident!! - Communication with FLYNAS is difficult: emails are either not answered or answered very slowly. You are dependent on expensive phone calls, which in my case only contained useless promises. To this day (5 months), the damage to my suitcase has not been compensated!! Airlines usually have insurance for such cases. Other airlines resolve these issues much faster. I feel that FLYNAS wants to shirk its responsibility and is therefore dragging out the process - hoping that the victim will give up. If there are such problems with something as minor as a suitcase damage, I don't want to know/experience how they behave in case of extreme delays, flight cancellations, or similar situations. One thing is for sure - that was my first and last flight with FLYNAS. If you don't want to be left without help when problems arise - I can only recommend avoiding FLYNAS.

Une excellente expérience de voyage ! Nous avons voyagé avec Saudia Airline pour notre aller-retour à Dubaï, avec une escale à Jeddah à l’aller et Riyad au retour.

Avant de partir, j’avais lu plusieurs commentaires négatifs, mais franchement, rien à redire ! Nous avons été très bien accueillis, le personnel était aux petits soins tout au long du vol. Des coussins, des plaids étaient fournis pour plus de confort, et la quantité de nourriture servie était plus que suffisante.

Les vols étaient à l’heure, et nous sommes même arrivés en avance, ce qui est toujours appréciable.

Tout s’est parfaitement déroulé, aucun problème, que du positif ! Je n’hésiterai pas à voyager à nouveau avec cette compagnie.
translated_text:
An excellent travel experience! We traveled with Saudia Airline for our round trip to Dubai, with a stopover in Jeddah on the way there and Riyadh on the way back.

Before leaving, I had read several negative comments, but frankly, I have nothing to complain about! We were very well received, the staff was attentive throughout the flight. Cushions and blankets were provided for extra comfort, and the amount of food served was more than sufficient.

The flights were on time, and we even arrived early, which is always appreciated.

Everything went perfectly, no problems, all positive! I will not hesitate to travel again with this company.

Instead, they told me it wasn’t their fault and that, according to their terms and conditions, customers just have to "manage" with whatever they provide.

To make matters worse, they said customers should not contact them directly and should only speak to their booking agents. This is completely unacceptable—why should an airline not take responsibility for the flights they operate? I ended up paying my agent extra just to reschedule my flight a day earlier, forcing me to rearrange everything at the last moment. It was stressful, costly, and a complete nightmare.

If you value your time, money, and peace of mind, stay far away from Saudia Airlines. Their customer service is unhelpful, and they take no responsibility for the inconvenience they cause. If anything goes wrong, you’re on your own. Instead, book with airlines that respect their customers and offer proper support when issues arise. Don’t let Saudia ruin your travel plans like they did mine!

raw_text:
PEGGIOR VIAGGIO DELLA MIA VITA A causa di overbooking del volo Jeddah- Jakarta del 25 Luglio 2025 io ed altre persone non abbiamo potuto prendere il volo, rimanendo bloccati a Jeddah per tutta la notte.
Lo staff di Saudia nell’aeroporto di Jeddah ci ha indirizzato in un volo del giorno dopo (e ci ha assicurato che avremmo avuto un rimborso del biglietto della tratta Jeddah-Jakarta di almeno il 150%), ma a causa del gravissimo ritardo, abbiamo perso la coincidenza del volo che avremmo avuto successivamente, Jakarta-Denpasar a causa del loro disguido e abbiamo dovuto acquistare un nuovo biglietto, (dal costo di 104€), e abbiamo dovuto trovarci un hotel a nostre spese per la notte a Jakarta, ci hanno garantito che anche questi sarebbero stati rimborsati.
Di conseguenza abbiamo perso ANCHE una notte già pagata nella casa prenotata a Bali.
A fine vacanza abbiamo scritto alla compagnia utilizzando il form indicato sul loro sito, come suggerito dai dipendenti di suadia, che prometteva una risposta entro tot settimane. Sono passati mesi e ancora NESSUNA risposta, servizio clienti TERRIBILE, se chiamate vi dicono che non sanno niente e l'unica cosa che si può fare è aspettare che rispondano via mail (cosa che non fanno).
In sintesi: volo spostato di un giorno intero, coincidenza persa, hotel a nostre spese, aerei vecchi e spesso sedili con vari problemi, E NESSUN RIMBORSO nonostante le promesse fatte.
PEGGIOR VIAGGIO DELLA MIA VITA, NON USATE QUESTA COMPAGNIA.
translated_text: WORST TRIP OF MY LIFE Due to overbooking of the Jeddah-Jakarta flight on July 25, 2025, I and other people could not take the flight, being stuck in Jeddah all night. Saudia staff at Jeddah airport directed us to a flight the next day (and assured us we would get a refund of at least 150% of the Jeddah-Jakarta ticket), but due to the severe delay, we missed the connecting flight Jakarta-Denpasar because of their mistake and had to buy a new ticket (costing 104€), and we had to find a hotel at our own expense for the night in Jakarta, which they guaranteed would also be reimbursed. Consequently, we also lost a night already paid for in the house booked in Bali. At the end of the vacation, we wrote to the company using the form on their website, as suggested by Saudia employees, which promised a response within a few weeks. Months have passed and still NO response, TERRIBLE customer service, if you call they say they know nothing and the only thing you can do is wait for an email reply (which they never send). In summary: flight delayed by a whole day, missed connection, hotel at our expense, old planes and often seats with various problems, AND NO REFUND despite promises made. WORST TRIP OF MY LIFE, DO NOT USE THIS COMPANY.

raw_text:
إحباط كبير عندما حجزت تذكرتي على الخطوط السعودية، بصفتها الناقل الوطني لدولة خليجية مرموقة، لم يتسلل إلى ذهني للحظة أنني سأعيش تجربة بهذا القدر من السوء. لقد اضطررت لحجز رحلة (عمان - جدة) عبر الرياض (ترانزيت)، وكانت النتيجة سلسلة من الإخفاقات التي لا تُغتفر:
• تخبط المواعيد: قامت الشركة بتغيير موعد الرحلة، مما أدى لتأخري ساعة كاملة عن الوصول لجدة، وهو ما ترتب عليه إلغاء حجز السيارة التي كانت بانتظاري لنقلي إلى مكة المكرمة.
• معاناة الترانزيت: رغم أن حجزي "ترانزيت"، إلا أنني صُدمت في الرياض بضرورة ختم دخول السعودية، واستلام الحقائب، ثم استقلال حافلة لمدة 20 دقيقة للوصول إلى صالة الرحلات الداخلية والقيام بإجراءات التسجيل (check-in) من جديد! هذا التخبط جعلني في حالة ركض مستمر وضغط عصبي بسبب ضيق الوقت.
• الاستهتار بحقوق الركاب: تم تغيير مقعدي في رحلة الرياض-جدة رغم أنه مقعد مدفوع ثمنه مسبقاً! وعندما احتججت، كان رد الموظفة الواهي: "ربما المقعد مكسور!".. كيف لشركة كبرى أن تغير مقعداً مدفوعاً بذرائع غير منطقية؟!
• غياب الخدمات اللوجستية: عند وصولنا الرياض، نزلنا عبر السلالم إلى حافلة استغرقت 10 دقائق لتوصيلنا للمطار! أين الممرات والخراطيم المباشرة؟ هل تُركت للطيران الاقتصادي وحُرم منها ركاب الناقل الوطني؟!
• أسطول متهالك: الطائرات قديمة جداً وتفتقر لأدنى معايير الترفيه؛ فلا شاشات ولا حتى خدمة ترفيه عبر الهاتف. شعرت في رحلتي (عمان-الرياض) و(جدة-عمان) أنني عدت بالزمن إلى طائرات التسعينات.
• وجبات هزيلة: قائمة الطعام لا تليق أبداً باسم "السعودية"؛ فالاختيار محصور بين قطعة "بيتزا مارغريتا" صغيرة أو "مافن"! هل يعقل أن تكون هذه وجبة رحلة مدتها ساعتان ونصف بين عاصمتين؟ هذا المستوى لا نراه حتى في أفقر شركات الطيران الاقتصادي.
• فوضى بوابات الصعود: في رحلة العودة بجدة، تم تغيير بوابة الصعود فجأة وأنا في السوق الحرة إلى أبعد بوابة في المطار (15 دقيقة مشياً)، مما تسبب في حالة انزعاج ومشي سريع باتجاه البوابة الجديدة البعيدة.
• تدني مستوى النظافة: بدلاً من أغطية الرأس القماشية التي تُبدل لكل راكب، تستخدم الشركة أغطية جلدية ثابتة ومقززة تفتقر لأدنى معايير التعقيم والنظافة.
• ضعف الكفاءات: لغة مسؤول طاقم الطائرة والطيار الإنجليزية (عبر الإذاعة الداخلية) ضعيفة جداً ومليئة بأخطاء ساذجة لا تليق بموظفي ناقل وطني يمثل المملكة أمام العالم.
حقيقة هي تجربة لا ترقى حتى لمستوى الطيران "منخفض التكاليف"، ولن أكرر تجربة السفر عبر هذه الخطوط مجدداً ما دام هذا هو مستوى خدماتها.
translated_text: Great frustration when I booked my ticket on Saudi Airlines, as the national carrier of a prestigious Gulf country, it never crossed my mind that I would have such a bad experience. I had to book a flight (Amman - Jeddah) via Riyadh (transit), and the result was a series of unforgivable failures: • Schedule confusion: The company changed the flight time, causing me to arrive an hour late in Jeddah, which led to the cancellation of the car reservation that was waiting to take me to Mecca. • Transit suffering: Although my booking was 'transit', I was shocked in Riyadh to have to stamp entry into Saudi Arabia, collect luggage, then take a 20-minute bus to the domestic terminal and check in again! This confusion made me run continuously and caused nervous stress due to tight timing. • Disregard for passenger rights: My seat on the Riyadh-Jeddah flight was changed even though it was prepaid! When I complained, the staff's weak response was: 'Maybe the seat is broken!' How can a major company change a paid seat with illogical excuses?! • Lack of logistics: Upon arrival in Riyadh, we descended via stairs to a bus that took 10 minutes to reach the airport! Where are the direct corridors and jet bridges? Are they left only for budget airlines and denied to the national carrier's passengers?! • Worn-out fleet: The planes are very old and lack basic entertainment standards; no screens or even phone entertainment service. I felt on my flights (Amman-Riyadh) and (Jeddah-Amman) like I went back to the 90s planes. • Meager meals: The menu is not worthy of the name 'Saudi'; the choice was limited to a small 'Margherita pizza' or a 'muffin'! Is this a meal for a two and a half hour flight between capitals? This level is not even seen in the poorest budget airlines. • Boarding gate chaos: On the return flight in Jeddah, the boarding gate was suddenly changed while I was in the duty-free to the farthest gate in the airport (15 minutes walk), causing discomfort and a fast walk to the new distant gate. • Poor cleanliness: Instead of cloth headrest covers changed for each passenger, the company uses fixed leather covers that are disgusting and lack basic sterilization and cleanliness standards. • Weak competencies: The English of the cabin crew leader and pilot (via internal radio) is very poor and full of silly mistakes unworthy of employees of a national carrier representing the Kingdom to the world. Honestly, this experience does not even reach the level of 'low-cost' flying, and I will not repeat traveling with this airline as long as this is their service level.

Review of Saudi Airlines Unfortunately, my experience at Jeddah Airport was extremely disappointing due to consistently poor service and unprofessional, condescending attitudes from staff.

I have travelled through many international airports around the world, and the level of rudeness and lack of professionalism displayed here stood out in the worst possible way. From the very beginning at the baggage check-in area, which was part of Saudi Airlines operations, the female staff responsible for weighing luggage were dismissive, rude, and spoke in a condescending manner, with no patience or courtesy shown.

The situation was made even more distressing as I was travelling with two elderly family members both grandmothers who required assistance. The staff assisting with luggage and wheelchairs were extremely rude, made inappropriate comments, and created an uncomfortable and unpleasant environment. Elderly passengers deserve care, respect, and compassion, none of which were shown.

Instead of feeling supported and welcomed, the overall atmosphere felt hostile and unnecessarily stressful. It is genuinely sad that an airport of this scale and importance, in partnership with a national airline, offers such poor service and displays such a lack of basic customer care.

An international airport especially one serving millions of pilgrims, families, and travellers each year should uphold far higher standards. This experience was unacceptable and has left a very negative impression. I sincerely hope both airport management and Saudi Airlines take staff conduct, training, and accountability seriously so that no other travellers, particularly the elderly, are subjected to this kind of treatment.

raw_text: Avoid using Saudia Airlines, they do not care about their customers Really disappointed with the service received by the Saudia Airlines last week. On Thursday 1st January, our 8.30am flight SV101 eventually got cancelled after waiting 3 hours on the plane. Most of the Saudia airport staff were unhelpful and very dismissive. All we wanted to know was what was going on as I was traveling with 2 young children and was in the airport from 5am with no sleep. Eventually we were taken to a hotel and was told that the coach would pick us up the next day at 4am to take us back to the airport for our rescheduled 9am flight. The coach did turn up and took the 1st batch off passengers but did not return to pick the rest of us up (approximately 50 passengers). We waited and waited and eventually had no choice but to arrange our own transport, causing more anxiety and stress. It was my our first time flying with Saudia and I was really shocked and disappointed with the level of service. We won't be traveling with them again anytime soon. I would advise people to use other airlines like Qatar Airways if possible.
