raw_text:
Half the Flight Without Luggage, No Support from Flynas Extremely poor baggage handling, zero accountability, and ineffective customer service. Travel with caution.

I travelled with Flynas on 3 January 2026 from Cairo to Kuwait via Jeddah (Flights XY-566 and XY-529). Two checked bags did not arrive, and despite filing an official PIR at Kuwait Airport, there was no meaningful update, no timeline, and no proactive communication for over 24 hours.

What made this experience unacceptable was not just the missing baggage, but the complete failure of customer service and the ground staff responsible for baggage handling and delivery. There was no coordination, no ownership, and no clear point of contact. Passengers are left to chase information endlessly.

Mishandled baggage can happen — but silence, poor communication, and lack of responsibility should not. Based on this experience, I strongly advise travellers to think twice before flying Flynas, especially if you are checking in luggage.

Definitely recommend When I first booked my holiday, I hadn't actually looked into or heard of Saudia so when I then looked up their reviews, I was hugely worried and nearly cancelled the tickets as I was expecting it to be awful

We flew from London to Dubai, with a layover in Jeddah and Riyadh on the respective legs

I did need to speak to Customer Services before travelling and also found them very helpful - though would recommend calling as their website is quite glitchy

Overall, we had a great experience with them and would definitely fly with them again - particularly as they were significantly cheaper than the equivalent Emirates flights (who we've flown with previously) and we didn't notice any real difference

raw_text: Terrible experience with Saudia . We had our trip interrupted due to denied boarding on our Rome Fiumicino-Nairobi flight, with a stopover in Jeddah, at 3:10 PM on December 21, 2025, operated by Saudia, despite having been to the gate on time (before all other passengers) and having a valid passport and visa issued by the Kenyan authorities. The visa was requested at the end of September, specifying the travel dates on which we were supposed to be in Kenya (December 22-December 28, 2025). These dates are listed on the document itself, issued on September 24, 2025, valid for 90 days and renewable for another 90 days upon arrival in Nairobi. We also requested a Denied Boarding Statement from Saudia, as we are entitled to request under EC Regulation 261/2004, but it was denied. During all this, we contacted the Kenyan immigration office, and they promptly responded on the official number +................ confirming that the visa was still valid on December 22nd, the day after our flight. Therefore, we believe it is right to give Saudia Airlines a negative review for their terrible and unprofessional handling of passengers, who deserve a little respect considering they lost not only the money for their flights but also their vacation.

raw_text:
⭐️ ABSOLUTELY AVOID SAUDIA GROUND STAFF: A Tale of Disastrous Service, Visa Denial, and Delayed Baggage (Flight Ref: 92H The ONLY Positive: Excellent In-Flight Experience
It is important to state clearly: my experience onboard Saudia's flights was top-notch.

Cabin and Comfort: The flights were clean, the seats were good, and the overall flight quality was high.

Service: The food was great, and the in-flight staff and pilots were excellent, professional, and attentive.

However, this positive experience was completely and utterly ruined by the unprofessional, incompetent, and frankly depressing service provided by Saudia's ground staff at both Jeddah and London, leading to significant financial loss and distress during my Umrah trip.

Issue 1: Stopover Visa SCAM & Financial Loss at Jeddah
I specifically booked with Saudia because of the highly advertised Stopover Visa Programme and I had paid for the associated insurance.

Misinformation: My initial check-in assured me the visa would be processed in Jeddah.

Ground Staff Failure: Upon arrival in Jeddah, the ground staff was clueless about their own airline's flagship visa programme.

Rude Supervisor: I was forced to wait 30-40 minutes for a supervisor who was shockingly rude. He flatly denied the existence of the Stopover Visa, refused to look at my evidence, and said his job was only for check-ins and upgrades.

Result: Due to this incompetence and lack of customer support, I was forced to pay again for a 1-year visa at immigration just for a 3-day Umrah visit, costing me money for the new visa, my family's parking, and precious time.

Issue 2: Racist and Rude Check-in at Jeddah (Jeddah to Dubai)
The service for my connection flight was equally appalling and felt discriminatory.

Excessive Wait: Despite Jeddah being Saudia's hub, the economy check-in line took over 1.5 hours due to inadequate staffing (only two counters open).

Prejudiced Agent: The check-in agent was immediately rude, impatient, and denied me check-in after seeing my Indian passport, demanding a UAE visa or residency.

Denial of Eligibility: He refused to check my passport or listen to my explanation that I was eligible for a Visa On Arrival due to possessing a valid US Visa and UK Residency. His conduct was discriminatory and completely unprofessional.

Resolution: The issue only resolved after a kind supervisor intervened and had to personally educate his staff on basic visa policies for eligible passengers.

Issue 3: Unannounced Baggage Delay and Lack of Care (Dubai to London)
My disastrous experience concluded with a major operational failure in London.

Zero Communication: I waited an hour at baggage collection, only to be told after everyone else had left that my bag was delayed due to "technical reasons"—a fact no one informed me of beforehand.

Distress & Health Risk: My bag contained essential clothing and medication, leaving me stranded in London empty-handed.

Customer Service Lie: The help desk initially promised evening delivery, only to suddenly change her story and say nothing would happen until after Monday because it was a weekend and no staff were working. She simply told me to call the airline.

Conclusion: A Pathetic Ground Experience
Saudia Airlines needs to understand that a great flight is worthless if the ground service is a hostile, disorganized mess. They failed to honor a paid-for service, their staff is untrained, rude, and potentially discriminatory, and their baggage management is incompetent.

Until Saudia radically overhauls its ground operations and customer service training, I strongly advise all travellers to avoid them.

raw_text:
Pathetic Experience My experience with Saudi Airlines on both inbound and outbound flights between Mumbai and Jeddah was extremely upsetting and unacceptable. The airline staff displayed rude, arrogant, and racially insensitive behaviour, especially towards passengers who required wheelchair assistance.

Despite requesting a wheelchair for an elderly passenger well in advance, no assistance was provided. The ground staff spoke in a condescending and dismissive manner, showing zero empathy or professionalism. Elderly passengers were left standing, confused, and helpless, while staff members responded with irritation instead of support.

What is even more disturbing is that this was not an isolated incident. The same rude attitude was shown to all passengers needing wheelchair assistance, clearly indicating a systemic problem and lack of respect for elderly and disabled travellers.

An international airline operating on religious and humanitarian values should be ashamed of such treatment. This behaviour reflects poor training, lack of accountability, and an underlying sense of discrimination. Passengers are not doing a favour by flying with your airline—they are paying customers who deserve dignity and basic human respect.

I strongly urge Saudi Airlines to:

Properly train staff in basic courtesy and sensitivity

Ensure wheelchair assistance is reliably provided

Investigate discriminatory behaviour on the Mumbai–Jeddah route

raw_text:
Sub-par customer service and lack of maintenance The Upgrade Nightmare

I successfully bid for a Business Class upgrade, received the invoice, and was charged. However, the technical integration was non-existent. My online check-in and seat selection continued to show Economy.

Pre-Flight Support: I contacted customer service multiple times 24 hours before departure. Each time, I was met with indifference. Agents refused to verify my status, repeatedly telling me I’d have to "find out at airport check-in."

The Layover Struggle: Even at the airport, the staff was unhelpful. Instead of resolving the clear discrepancy in their system, they prompted me to file a complaint form rather than looking into the issue. It took significant "convincing" and a hard-fought argument to get them to actually check my booking.

The Result: While I eventually got the seat I had already paid for, the process left me stressed and worried throughout my entire trip. A premium passenger should never have to fight this hard for a confirmed service.

Maintenance also seems to be an issue. On my grandmother's return flight, her Business Class seat had a broken tray, which is a basic oversight that shouldn't happen in a premium cabin, especially when traveling as a senior.

My issues aren't isolated to this flight. I have previously dealt with double-charging on a booking, and the recovery process was a bureaucratic black hole:

I visited the Riyadh office, called the service center, and filed online forms.
Every agent promised a resolution "in a few days," yet no one ever followed
The refund was never processed, despite months of chasing them through every available channel (sales team, online forms, and in-person visits).

Saudia’s ground staff and support agents seem dismissive and eager to redirect you to an online form rather than solving the problem in front of them. Unlike other leading airlines that prioritize passenger peace of mind, Saudia feels like a "file a ticket and hope for the best" operation. translated_text:
Sub-par customer service and lack of maintenance The Upgrade Nightmare

I successfully bid for a Business Class upgrade, received the invoice, and was charged. However, the technical integration was non-existent. My online check-in and seat selection continued to show Economy.

Pre-Flight Support: I contacted customer service multiple times 24 hours before departure. Each time, I was met with indifference. Agents refused to verify my status, repeatedly telling me I’d have to "find out at airport check-in."

The Layover Struggle: Even at the airport, the staff was unhelpful. Instead of resolving the clear discrepancy in their system, they prompted me to file a complaint form rather than looking into the issue. It took significant "convincing" and a hard-fought argument to get them to actually check my booking.

The Result: While I eventually got the seat I had already paid for, the process left me stressed and worried throughout my entire trip. A premium passenger should never have to fight this hard for a confirmed service.

Maintenance also seems to be an issue. On my grandmother's return flight, her Business Class seat had a broken tray, which is a basic oversight that shouldn't happen in a premium cabin, especially when traveling as a senior.

My issues aren't isolated to this flight. I have previously dealt with double-charging on a booking, and the recovery process was a bureaucratic black hole:

I visited the Riyadh office, called the service center, and filed online forms.
Every agent promised a resolution "in a few days," yet no one ever followed
The refund was never processed, despite months of chasing them through every available channel (sales team, online forms, and in-person visits).

Saudia’s ground staff and support agents seem dismissive and eager to redirect you to an online form rather than solving the problem in front of them. Unlike other leading airlines that prioritize passenger peace of mind, Saudia feels like a "file a ticket and hope for the best" operation. Until they fix their backend systems and train their staff to actually care about resolution, I would be hesitant to rely on them for high-stakes travel.

raw_text:
Refund and EC261 compensation wrongly refused – unacceptable handling Our flight SV210 from Milan Malpensa to Jeddah on 4 December 2025 was cancelled after boarding due to a technical issue. As a result, we missed our connecting flight to Jakarta (SV818), both flights being part of the same booking.

No alternative flight was offered and no refund was provided, in clear violation of Regulation (EC) No 261/2004.

Over several weeks, our claim was wrongly rejected, with responsibilities endlessly shifted between Guest Relations and Back Office. We were repeatedly told to “wait” or open new tickets. No one took ownership of the case.

Saudia repeatedly claimed the cancellation was due to “circumstances beyond the airline’s control,” which is factually incorrect: a technical issue is not an extraordinary circumstance under EC261.

Even the ticket refund has still not been processed, with illogical excuses provided each time. We are now forced to escalate the matter to the Italian Civil Aviation Authority (ENAC) and pursue legal action to recover both the refund and the €600 compensation per passenger.

Extremely frustrating and unprofessional experience. I strongly advise other passengers to be cautious regarding post-disruption support and respect of passenger rights.

raw_text:
Complaint Against Saudi Airlines Representative Khurram Khalid for Bribery and Abuse of Authority I missed my flight on 18 January, 2026 from Lahore to Jeddah and onward to London due to the unprofessional and unacceptable conduct of a senior representative of Saudi Arabian Airlines named Khurram Khalid.

I waited for more than three hours in long queues, first outside and then inside the airport. During this time, junior staff members, along with the senior representative Khurram Khalid, repeatedly asked me for money to process my check-in faster. They said in my local language, “kuch ley dey k baat khatam kren”. I did not have money to give them, so I refused.

When it was finally my turn at the counter and the counter staff asked for my passport, Khurram Khalid singled me out from the queue and instructed the counter staff to return my passport. This entire situation was deliberately created approximately one hour before my scheduled departure, at a time when I still had sufficient time to check in and board the flight.

I politely requested him and followed him across the floor, asking calmly why I was not being allowed to check in while all other passengers were being processed. He behaved in an egoistic and aggressive manner, clearly misusing his authority because he did not get what he wanted.

I then went to another counter where the staff again took my passport to check me in, but once more Khurram Khalid intervened, scolded the counter staff, and ordered him to return my passport. The counter staff had no choice but to obey and proceeded to check in the passenger behind me instead. I was told that only Khurram Khalid could resolve the issue, so I approached him again.

At this point, Khurram Khalid took my photograph without my consent and threatened me. This triggered me to start recording a video as evidence of how I was deliberately singled out, prevented from checking in, and caused to miss my flight. In the video, it can be clearly seen that the counter staff was willing to process my check-in, but the senior representative repeatedly stopped them and forced me to follow him around the airport to satisfy his ego.

He created unnecessary drama, and when I raised my voice in frustration, he reacted negatively. The most alarming part is that Khurram Khalid was carrying an expired ID card while exercising authority.

Horrible staff and horrible airline policies Our experience with Saudi airlines was horrible. Airline has horrible policies and their staff is extremely unhelpful and rude.

We had a flight from Toronto to Jeddah with a connection Jeddah to Medina. After that we were gonna take a flight from Makkah to Lahore with a connection in Dubai and lastly Lahore to Toronto. Flight from Toronto was 1 and a half hour late and we ended up missing our connecting flight to Jeddah. We have three kids and just taking them to washrooms after landing meant we barely made to the gate 10 minutes before the plane was supposed to fly. They closed the gates and sent us to one counter to another counter where all the staff said they can’t help and the system has cancelled all of our flights for the rest of the trip. Mind you that’s before the flight even took off. Apparently if we didn’t get on Jeddah flight, system thinks we have missed Toronto flight and won’t be taking rest of the flight which are days and weeks away. I wonder how the system didn’t pick on us getting on Toronto to Jeddah flight????!!!! It was so so disappointing and frustrating … and our frustration and inability to understand the system was frustrating for the staff??? They were passing rude comments and asking us to shut up (like literally a girl at guest service told me to shut up) and were just sending us to one counter to another without giving any proper direction. Airline doesn’t take any responsibility of their passengers travelling for Umarah and have no fear of Allah. Now we are stranded at the airport … trying to figure out what to do next with three kids and no where to go. This is just the Airlines way of making more money … now tickets are 1000s of dollars more expensive and staff is not even willing to find new tickets for us and telling us to work through our agent who has no access to their system and our original tickets anymore. Never in this life we will every travel through Saudi Airlines and I would advise to everyone to not be fooled by occasional price drop … they will make sure you miss one step and they can get you to pay much much more then you would have paid for any better airlines.

Schlechter Board Service Also unser Rückflug von DXB nach Amsterdam war über Jeddah Saudi Arabien.
Die Flugzeuge waren schon modern,aber der über beiden Flugzeuge war der Service reine Katastrophe.

translated_text: Poor onboard service Our return flight from DXB to Amsterdam was via Jeddah, Saudi Arabia. The planes were modern, but the service on both flights was a complete disaster. We each received only a small 200 ml bottle of water, and the food was not special. With the meal, you get a small drink and tea or coffee. On the second plane from Jeddah to Amsterdam, we asked why for a 6-7 hour flight you only get 200 ml of water. At the end, just before landing, after we said we would give feedback, the service tried to give us water. What use are modern planes if onboard service is such a disaster? With Saudi Airlines, you always have to beg for water, etc. Terrible. We will neither fly Saudi Airlines again nor recommend them. Also, at Jeddah airport, it is so cold that you end up catching a cold. We still have a cold today.

Formal Complaint Regarding Booking Date Error and Refusal to Assist Formal Complaint Regarding Booking Date Error and Refusal to Assist
I am writing to formally complain about a serious issue I experienced with my flight booking and the lack of support provided by your staff.
I booked my flight online for 7/1/2026, and I have clear proof from the booking website confirming this date. However, when I arrived at the airport, I was informed that my ticket was booked for 6/1/2026, which is incorrect.
Despite presenting valid proof of my online booking showing the correct date (7/1/2026), the airport staff refused to acknowledge the error or assist me in resolving the issue. They remained firm on their position without offering any solution or escalation.
As a result of this mistake:
I was treated as if I had missed my flight
I was forced to purchase a new ticket at my own expense
I arrived late at my destination
I faced a serious crisis at my workplace due to the delay
Additionally, the customer service at the airport was unhelpful. I was repeatedly told to call a customer service number, and I spent the entire day trying to reach someone for assistance. Unfortunately, no one helped resolve the issue, either at the airport or via customer support.
I later filed an official complaint requesting a refund for the additional ticket and compensation, as the issue was clearly caused by a system or booking error. However, my request was refused without a fair investigation, despite the evidence I provided.

raw_text:
Airline failed to honor my confirmed and issued ticket I booked a flight and selected my seat during booking. After payment, my ticket was confirmed and issued.
I received this confirmation email with the ticket number, seat specified all well 3 months in advance. Also received a reminder from the airline itself 1 week before departure.

Despite this, when I went to check in, I was told that my seat had been given to someone else and that my reservation could not be found. I had to show my receipt for the additional service (seat), ticket confirmation email to prove that I had a valid, paid, and issued ticket.

What happened next was honestly shocking. The case was escalated to a supervisor, who told me, “if you had come earlier, this wouldn’t have happened,” and ask me to decide whether I wanted to fly or not, even though I was already there well in advance and the delay was caused internally.

The supervisor also said it was not their responsibility because they did not personally make the change and done by someone else internally.

I could not believe what I heard and also being blamed for something I did not cause felt extremely unfair.

Once a ticket is issued, the airline is responsible for honoring it and they clearly failed.

A trip I had carefully planned in advance quickly turned into a nightmare.

SkyTeam Elite Plus and Saudia Gold status also made no difference. I would not recommend investing time in loyalty with an airline that can break trust this easily for its own convenience.

For a long-haul flight, this was a very stressful and disappointing experience. I raised complaint and still havent got the refund.

I am writing to formally file a complaint regarding a serious issue that occurred during my recent journey with Saudia Airlines.

During check-in, one of my hand-luggage items was taken from me to be placed in the aircraft hold. However, no baggage tag was issued or attached to this item, despite my asking for confirmation. Following the flight, the bag was not delivered and has been missing ever since, with no updates provided. I have repeatedly attempted to obtain information about its status, but the lack of response and clarity has been unacceptable.

This situation has caused considerable stress and inconvenience, especially because the bag contains important personal belongings. Given that the bag was taken from me at the check-in counter without a tag, I expect Saudia Airlines to treat this matter with urgency and conduct a thorough investigation.

raw_text:
Travel Chaos with Saudia Airline. My family and I travelled to Jeddah, the city where I grew up, on the 19th December. What should have been a meaningful and joyful trip turned into one of the most frustrating travel experiences of my life. We arrived at the airport in Riyadh by 7 a.m. for our 9 a.m. flight. Yet when we checked the departure boards, our flight wasn’t even listed. We waited — confused and anxious — for over an hour before discovering from the screen, not from staff, that our flight had been delayed. Then delayed again. And again. 11 a.m. became 12 p.m., then 1 p.m., with no explanation, no announcements, and no accountability. Eventually, a gate number appeared. There, we met dozens of other stranded passengers — many of whom had been waiting since 1 a.m., most since 4 a.m. — all equally uninformed. The few staff members we managed to find were completely unhelpful, offering nothing but vague responses and empty stares. No clarity. No honesty. No leadership. What made the situation even more infuriating was watching other airlines, including Flynas, operate normally while not a single Saudia flight departed. After more than 12 exhausting hours, we were finally informed — not by staff, but by an app — that our flight was cancelled. Collecting our luggage took yet another painfully long wait. When we finally reached customer service, we were met with a massive queue and, effectively, silence. I was initially denied the chance to speak to anyone at all. When I finally did, I asked one simple question: Why was the flight delayed? The response was cowardly and insulting: “Natural causes — therefore we are not liable for compensation.”
What makes this even worse is that Saudia’s own Customer Rights Policy states: 1 hour delay → refreshments 2 hours → a meal 6 hours → 150% ticket refund, rebooking, and hotel accommodation, etc. We received none of this - not even a glass of water. Instead, we were told to “go home and wait.” This experience revealed an airline built on empty promises, deflection, and dishonesty. The lack of transparency, care, and basic responsibility was shocking.

raw_text:
Saudi airline is the worst in the world On the 29th of October we were going to pakistan landing in lahore, but unfortunately we missed the flight as my daughter who is 5 years old had dihorria and we were constantly going to the toilet. My son who was 6 months old at the time also needed a nappy change at the time. We didnt realise that gate had closed cause usually they wait for passengers to come even after announcing the final boarding announcement. Our flight was due to take off at 5:20 and we got to the gate at 5:05. The aeroplane had not departed just yet and was still stood outside but took off after 5 minutes. Im not sure why the aeroplane took off at 5:10 when it was due to take off at 5:20, considering we had children and given our circumstances the staff could have allowed us to board as we still had time to departure. After that we were sent to the customer information desk, they asked us for the boarding passes and 4 out of 5 boarding passes did not mention that the gate closes 20 mins before departure. It was only written on my sons hamza saqibs boarding pass which we unfortunately did not see. We pressurised the staff to find us next available flights as i had a little baby and it was not possible for us to stay at the airport as we had limited formula and nappies and clothes. But they said that there's no flights available so we have to find our own. Eventually we managed to find a flight after a few hours. We asked the staff to give us our luggage, they said that the luggage is in the basement and it doesn't go on the flight if the passengers miss their flight but he ensured us that our luggage will be on the flight that we choose to go on. During the night the staff had changed and another team member took over the other staff member, when we told him we found another flight and mentioned our luggage he said that he could not promise that our luggage will go on the flight as we gave short notice and usually they need 2- 4 hours notice. I stated that I have a small baby who is 6 months. His essentials were in the suitcases so it was important that the luggage comes with us. He said that he try his best but cannot promise that all the luggage will board with us.
My wife and children then got on the flight that we had booked, there was no space for me so I had to wait 24 hours to get on the next available flight. The flight which we was able to get was taking us to the Islamabad airport in pakistan so when my wife and children landed they went to collect the luggage they waited an hour to get the luggage but the luggage never came. My wife then reported it to the missing baggage department. They said to my wife that our luggage is most likely at the lahore airport but to confirm this they took our details and phone number and stated that they will give us a call. In the afternoon we get a call saying that it is at the lahore airport. We was not happy about this because the staff at jeddah airport had lied to us that our luggage is at jeddah and has not boarded even though we asked them so many times to confirm and they still kept lying even though they knew we had little children. This was really unprofessional of them and we were not happy about this. We had to wait 2 days to get our luggage considering we have a baby. These people don't care about anyone they need some qualified staff and soo rude they are.
Bycott saudi airline

raw_text: Estafa Stopover Visa Atención al cliente pésima. Antes de empezar el viaje, intenté sacar el stopover visa para poder visitar Riad, y al llegar al método de pago, el sistema no lo tramitaba. Después de intercambiar numerosas llamadas y correos, empecé el viaje sin tener una visa para Riad. Cuando llegué a Riad, me obligaron a pagar la Visa On Arrival, ya que al hacer escala pasaba de una terminal internacional a una local. Ahí hablé con el director que había en el mostrador de la aerolínea y me dijo que reclamara de forma online. Puse la reclamación con muchas dificultades, ya que esa parte de la web también dejaba de funcionar durante el trámite, y todavía sigo esperando una respuesta de la compañía. Si queréis volar con ellos y hacer un stopover, tened en cuenta que acabaréis pagando 100€ más por la visa y no os lo devolverán, aunque prometan en su web una tarifa reducida.
translated_text: Scam Stopover Visa Poor customer service. Before starting the trip, I tried to get the stopover visa to be able to visit Riyadh, and when I reached the payment method, the system did not process it. After exchanging numerous calls and emails, I started the trip without having a visa for Riyadh. When I arrived in Riyadh, I was forced to pay the Visa On Arrival, since when making a stopover I was moving from an international terminal to a local one. There I spoke with the manager at the airline counter and he told me to file a claim online. I filed the claim with great difficulty, as that part of the website also stopped working during the process, and I am still waiting for a response from the company. If you want to fly with them and make a stopover, keep in mind that you will end up paying 100€ more for the visa and they will not refund it, even though they promise a reduced rate on their website.

None stop noise I’m leaving two stars because the flight attendants were professional and courteous, and the food and beverage service was excellent. However, the flight itself was very uncomfortable due to excessive noise from several families with young children. The situation was not well managed, and the constant crying and shouting made it difficult to rest or enjoy the long-haul journey.
Additionally, the condition of one of the lavatories was unacceptable, as some passengers did not maintain proper hygiene standards. This seems to occur more frequently on certain long routes where large groups travel together, and I encourage the airline to enforce better cleanliness and cabin etiquette.
While I appreciate the kindness of the crew, the overall experience was far from relaxing, and I would not be flying with this airline again.

Sicher und komfortabel ans Ziel Alle Flüge waren pünktlich. Die gereichten Speisen an Bord waren ausreichend und von sehr guter Qualität. Bordpersonal freundlich, kundenorentiert und sehr gepflegt.
Fühlte mich jederzeit sicher und gut betreut an Bord. Es gibt keine alkoholischen Getränke an Bord, dafür leckere Säfte. Umsteigen in Djeddah problemlos und schnell, sehr angenehmer 'ruhiger' Flughafen mit zahlreichen Sitzmöglichkeiten. Sehr gutes Preis-Leistungs-Verhältnis.
Achtung: Der Flex Tarif erlaubt zwar die Umbuchung, im Falle einer Umbuchung gehen aber sämtliche Sitzplatzreservierungen verloren, auch für die nicht umgebuchte Strecke.
Sitzplatzreservierungen für alle Strecken müssen erneut gebucht und noch einmal bezahlt werden!
Schade dass man auf keinem der Flüge eine Begrüssung aus dem Cockpit gehört hat.
translated_text: Safe and comfortable to the destination All flights were on time. The meals served on board were sufficient and of very good quality. Cabin crew friendly, customer-oriented, and very well-groomed. I always felt safe and well cared for on board. There are no alcoholic beverages on board, but delicious juices instead. Transfers in Jeddah were easy and quick, a very pleasant 'quiet' airport with numerous seating options. Very good value for money. Note: The Flex fare allows rebooking, but in case of rebooking, all seat reservations are lost, even for the non-rebooked segment. Seat reservations for all segments must be booked again and paid for again! It's a pity that on none of the flights was there a greeting from the cockpit.

raw_text:
Deeply Disappointed by Disrespectful Behavior from Cabin Crew Member “Ahmad” – Flyadeal F3829 (Jeddah → Karachi, 30 Nov I am writing this as the son of a 63-year-old passenger who experienced extremely rude and unacceptable behavior from one of Flyadeal’s cabin crew members, “Ahmad,” on Flight F3829 from Jeddah to Karachi on 30th November 2025 (1:05 AM departure).

My father, who has genuine medical issues, was carrying a slightly heavy hand-carry bag and needed assistance placing it into the overhead compartment. He politely asked the crew member, Ahmad, for help. Instead of offering basic courtesy—let alone compassion for an elderly passenger—Ahmad bluntly replied:

“It’s not my job.”

My father calmly explained that he had a medical condition and genuinely could not lift the bag. Ahmad’s response was shockingly heartless:

“That’s not my problem.”

When my father, feeling humiliated, said he would file a complaint, Ahmad dismissed him again, saying:

“Do whatever you want and sit down on your seat.”

As his son, watching my father—an elderly man who rarely asks anyone for help—be treated with such disrespect and arrogance was absolutely heartbreaking. No passenger should ever be spoken to like this, especially someone older with medical limitations. Cabin crew are trained to ensure safety and basic human decency onboard, and this behavior was the opposite of everything Flyadeal claims to stand for.

We are frequent travelers and have never encountered such dismissive, unprofessional conduct from any airline staff. This experience left my father upset and embarrassed and left our entire family extremely disappointed in Flyadeal’s service standards.

I sincerely hope the airline reviews this incident seriously, retrains or disciplines the staff member involved, and ensures no other elderly or medically challenged passenger is treated this way again.

Respect and empathy cost nothing—but to vulnerable passengers, they mean everything.

raw_text:
Extremely Poor Customer Service and Unfair Handling by Flynas Unfortunately, my experience with Flynas was one of the worst travel experiences I have ever had, and I will never fly with them again.

My flight from Dammam to Jeddah on 21 / … at 9:30 AM was cancelled by the airline. As a result, I was forced to rebook another flight at 1:55 AM, arriving in Jeddah at 4:20 AM instead of the originally scheduled 12:35 PM arrival.

This cancellation caused significant inconvenience and financial loss, including:
• Having to check into the hotel very early and pay for an extra night.
• Cancelling my original train booking and purchasing a new ticket due to the change in arrival time.
• A complete disruption to my travel plans, which were all based on the original flight schedule.

What made the situation even worse was the extremely poor customer service. When I contacted Flynas via WhatsApp, the customer service agent ended the conversation abruptly while I was explaining the issue and provided no real assistance. Emails were ignored, and it took four phone calls before someone finally answered.

Despite the cancellation being entirely Flynas’ responsibility, I was told that I was at fault and was asked to pay fees to change my return flight, which is completely unreasonable.

A professional airline should take responsibility for its own cancellations and support its passengers properly. Instead, I experienced unhelpful staff, long response times, and a lack of accountability.

Rating: 1/5
I do not recommend Flynas and will not be flying with them again.

raw_text:
“Faced seat mismanagement and rude, threatening crew behavior on Saudia Airline SV734 when asking for my rightful seat. I usually don’t post complaints publicly, but my recent experience on Saudia Arline SV734 dated 06 Nov 2025 was deeply concerning and needs attention.
From the moment I boarded, there was complete mismanagement of seating. My assigned seat was occupied, and despite standing for a long time, no crew member assisted. I was later moved to a temporary seat right in front of the washroom—uncomfortable, noisy, and unpleasant. I accepted it only to help the flight depart on time.
After takeoff, when I politely requested to return to my original seat due to discomfort, a staff member shockingly responded with yelling, threats, and aggressive behavior. I was even told security would be called—simply for asking for the seat printed on my boarding pass.
When I requested the supervisor, the situation became even worse. He was dismissive, rude, and repeatedly said he “couldn’t do anything.” At one point, he even told me to “do whatever you want.” Only after I firmly insisted did he finally check my boarding pass and return me to my rightful seat.
This was not just poor service—it was unprofessional, intimidating, and completely unacceptable from a national airline that represents the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.
I am sharing this not for sympathy, but to highlight the urgent need for accountability, staff training, and respect for passengers. I hope Saudia takes this seriously and ensures no one else goes through such treatment.

raw_text:
Cheap, budget standard airline. I would look at the alternatives before using this airline again.

The Good: it’s cheap and I got to and from my destination.

The Bad: Why didn’t the airline tell me that in Pakistan, checking in online means nothing? At passport control, after queuing for half an hour, I was sent back to the Check-in desk to collect physical boarding cards!!

We were called to board in both directions, then had to stand in a queue for 15 minutes? Why? Why not call people to board when you are ready to board? Yesterday, when we did get to ‘board’ we were then held at the plane entrance a further 10 minutes. The old man at the front could clearly not walk well and was propped up by his aging wife - was it really so hard for Saudi staff to spot the issue and to get the man a seat? Maybe if they weren’t on their mobiles 90% of the time?

Riyadh has to be one of the worst airports to have a long stopover in. Ridiculously bright, seats designed to stop you getting comfy and gave a nap, nowhere to get your head down, and one out of hundreds of charging points that worked - seriously? Nearly every one was broken, had the fuse missing or were disabled. I found one socket working, in a corner in the upstairs food hall. This is appalling management.

The in-flight food was inedible. It really was poor stodgy rubbish.

My return flight had many free seats. Yet I was jammed in to where there were lots of very noisy young children. The attendants could see there was a problem and could gave at least offered to move some of us desperate to sleep.

My biggest complaint are the seats. They are seriously uncomfortable, using slippery fake leather with little legroom and a poor recline. Sleeping was all but impossible. I was totally exhausted and exasperated by the time I got off the flight.

Unreliable Baggage Handling and Unhelpful Customer Service Experience I am very disappointed with the level of customer service I received from Saudia Airlines.

My baggage was offloaded without my knowledge, even though a customer service agent clearly told me that it would arrive at my destination. When I discovered this, I tried to file a baggage claim, but the online claim form does not work properly — it keeps loading endlessly. I also contacted customer service several times, but no one was able to provide real assistance or a clear solution.

It’s unacceptable that such a major airline has such poor communication and unreliable support, especially when passengers are already dealing with stressful travel issues. Saudia Airlines needs to improve its customer service systems, ensure accurate information is given to passengers, and provide timely help when things go wrong.

I truly hope this feedback is taken seriously and leads to better service for future travelers.

raw_text: Criminal, despicable airline Criminal, corrupt airline. Layers and layers of egregiousness with this horrible company. Cancelled our flights back to the US from Cairo twice, with the second cancellation accompanying a rebooking for nearly TWO WEEKS later, with no alternative flight options. Effectively stranded us in the Mideast with two young children. To make matters worse, the airline provides absolutely no way to contact them while overseas, despite assistance from our Cairo hotel staff, etc. We spent hours searching for a phone number, email address, Whatsapp number, anything, to no avail. Finally, we got a family member in the US to contact them on our behalf, and after multiple phone calls and being given inconsistent information, our fam member was told that the airline would not rebook us on any earlier flights (though they existed both through this airline and sister airlines), and that they could only cancel and offer other options if we paid out of pocket. As if the shock of this approach wasn't enough, when we decided we had no choice but to opt for the cancellation option and rebook ourselves, Saudia Air initially said we could cancel directly with them over the phone for the refund, but when our fam member attempted to do that, they pivoted and said we had to go through Expedia, because the flights had been booked through them initially. Desperate for a resolution, we ultimately paid out of pocket to get ourselves out of Egypt through a different airline, and now, nearly two weeks later, both we and Expedia are still battling this despicable airline for the refund of our cancelled flights and they are being completely unresponsive on all fronts. We are in the process of taking legal action against this airline, filing complaints with the DOT, and have also involved our credit card company. As an addon, if we had waited in Egypt until their proposed date of nearly two weeks after our scheduled flight out, we'd have now been stuck there given the current Mideast conflict, which began after we left the country. Do yourself a favor and avoid Saudia Air like the plague. Their actions essentially amount to criminality/theft in the US.

raw_text:
Vicious Circle or DREADFUL experience with "Flynas" Customer Service online, via email, on WhatsApp and on the phone Vicious Circle, DREADFUL EXPERIENCE with "Flynas" company's CUSTOMER SERVICE (the company has no concern for its customers outside of Jeddah or Riyadh)
March 2026
My review is going to be a VERY NEGATIVE REVIEW for "Flynas" CUSTOMER SERVICE though I am an understanding and considerate person aware of the issues around. If I could give less points, I would have!
"Flynas"'s MAIN target seems to be a manipulation of the customers to gain the maximum profit of moderate income passengers. They skillfully ignored my messages, emails, phone calls , WhatsApp messaging to get a profit of 1848 Riyals without providing ANY SERVICE, and I still have not received the rest of 160 Riyals. I could not by any means to connect to the CS NO MATTER HOW HARD I TRIED FOR 4 DAYS to modify my ticket in order not to lose my hard-earned expensive ticket money (which they call Light Flex.). Alongside that, I contacted Flynas website's Ticket Return online, and I received a message" contact customer service" It was a VICIOUS CIRCLE to make me lose my ticket money!
They are selling their tickets all over Saudi Arabia, and have offices only in Riyadh and Jeddah, and the management cares less about what happens with its customers, as their CS is NON RESPONSIVE especially for English speakers. It was an extremely stressful and anxious experience for me and I advise everybody to STAY AWAY from this airlines which calls itself "the leading low cost airline" in the Middle east having 0 CUSTOMER SERVICE for customers who live outside of Riyadh and Jeddah. Also, they expropriated the luggage from me on the previous flight. First their Airport Control claimed I should pay 200 Riyals for the overweight luggage, and when I approached the check-in counter, the amount went up to 750 Riyals which I did not have with me at that point. After that they claimed they provide my 14 kg full bag of goodies on return, but when I messaged, called and then visited the local airport police department locker where they should have kept the luggage, they did not have my beautiful brown bag, and I lost all my stuff and my textbooks needed for my work. The representative who ignored my messages and calls and requests of help, A.N . works for "Saudia", and who was overlooking "Flynas" at that moment. Also, the "Flynas" airport general manager M.G. gave false promises being polite but not providing ANY assistance in any matter.
Flynas is really a manipulative, promiscuous and ONLY profit-oriented private company which is targeted for income ONLY. STAY AWAY PLEASE
FYI "Saudia" is much better.

raw_text:
Personnel irrespectueux ! Hormis quel que exception Franchement, mon expérience avec le personnel de Saudia Airlines à l’aéroport de Djeddah a été catastrophique. Un niveau de désagrément rarement vu : attitude froide, méprisante, et aucun sens du service ! Le personnel quitte son poste d'enregistrement de bagage en laissant une file d'attente pleine et ne reviens plus !!!

Le pire reste le traitement réservé à certains passagers, notamment africains, qui m’a profondément choqué. On a clairement l’impression d’un manque total de respect, avec des comportements et des paroles qui frôlent (voire dépassent) les limites de l’acceptable. C’est humiliant et inadmissible en 2026.

Dans un aéroport international, on s’attend à un minimum de professionnalisme et d’humanité. Là, c’est tout l’inverse : aucune considération, aucune politesse, et une impression très marquée de discrimination.

Je déconseille fortement cette compagnie si vous tenez à être traité avec dignité. Une telle attitude ne devrait tout simplement pas exister dans le secteur du transport aérien.
translated_text: Disrespectful staff! Except for a few exceptions, frankly, my experience with Saudia Airlines staff at Jeddah airport was catastrophic. A level of unpleasantness rarely seen: cold attitude, contemptuous, and no sense of service! The staff leaves their baggage check-in post leaving a full queue and never returns!!! The worst is the treatment reserved for some passengers, especially Africans, which deeply shocked me. There is clearly a total lack of respect, with behaviors and words that border (or even exceed) the limits of acceptability. It is humiliating and unacceptable in 2026. In an international airport, one expects a minimum of professionalism and humanity. Here, it is the opposite: no consideration, no politeness, and a very marked impression of discrimination. I strongly discourage this company if you want to be treated with dignity. Such an attitude simply should not exist in the air transport sector.

raw_text:
Underrated Airlines with great Business Class experience The service onboard this leg was good, with a very attentive cabin crew. Despite not having smiles or striking conversation, she was observed to be attentive, and I felt very well taken care of by her. Although the service on board seemed to be taken from a page of the airline's SOP and executed by a very well-oiled crew, I could feel the attentiveness of the cabin crew working on my aisle. Throughout the flight, the cabin crew was kept busy with the different meal times. And when she walked by and saw that I was still awake, she offered to serve my light meal. After clearing my table, the same cabin crew came over to my seat with a tray of snacks, and at the same time, checked if I wanted any drinks to go along. During breakfast time, the cabin crew, seeing that I was awake, quickly heated up my breakfast order and served it after seeing I was settled down. The attentive cabin crew quickly cleared my table. We were presented with a retro white Saudia-branded amenity kit shortly after boarding. Inside the amenity kit, I found the Norwegian-branded Sprekenhus products such as lip balm, facial mist and hand cream, alongside eyemasks, dental kits and Saudia-branded socks.

Saudia houses all its 24 Business Class passengers in a single cabin onboard its B787-10 aircraft. Saudia B787 Business Class cabin looks clean and classy. The cream-coloured high-back seats bring out the black used in the framing of the IFE screen, while the dual-tone sand colour used in the inviting leather seat pays homage to the desert landscape in the kingdom, and the wood-grained finish on the seat's tables exudes a sense of luxury. The earth colour used in the carpet and the interior of the seat back throughout the cabin makes one feel relaxed. White walls and the bright lighting during boarding accentuate the cabin's clean look. There are three lavatories, one in the front of the aircraft and two at the back between the Business Class and Economy Class cabins. The dark brown used in the flooring and the cabinetry in the lavatory gave it a distinct, posh look. The lavatory was stocked with Saudia's in-house Eau de Parfum, hand lotion, and facial mist.

Business Class seats onboard Saudia B787-10 aircraft are arranged in a 1-2-1 configuration. The dual-tone sand-coloured leather seat has a generous seat pitch of 78” and measures 21” wide. The aisle side armrest can be lowered to give the seat a more generous width. Like most Business Class seats, these onboard Saudia B787 seats come in three standard configurations—Take-off/landing, lounge, and bed modes —which can be activated using the touchscreen seat control panel. The seat felt comfortable even in its most uncomfortable upright position for take-off/landing, thanks to the thick padding and the soft leather used in these seats. In bed mode, the seat can recline to 180°, turning it into a comfortable flatbed on which we had a great sleep during the flight. Despite not having any doors in these seats, I still find a good amount of privacy in bed mode thanks to the high back of the seat. The lounge mode sits between the upright and flatbed modes, which are angled to provide passenger comfort. I like how the angles in these seats can be further adjusted, giving passengers the freedom to customise the seat angle to their preferences.

I thought the storage in the Business Class seats onboard Saudia B787-10 aircraft was very generous. Right in the adjustable armrest on the aisle side of the seat lies a small compartment, large enough for the airlines to stow the provided headphones or bottled water. The main storage is found under the side table between the two seats (for those sitting in the middle couple seats) or at the window. There is one large compartment beneath the side table on the Business Class seat. The IFE controller and the charging outlets are placed above the side table at the seat. There is another storage area in the small cubby near the calf area inside the seat. I was also able to use the space under the footwell to place my carry-on. Saudia B787-10 aircraft Business Class is fitted with a large wooden table. It is even useful when stored under the IFE screen, with ⅔ of the bi-fold table sticking out, giving us a good amount of space to place our drinks. The table, when fully deployed, is rather sizeable and sturdy, which is perfect for meals or for doing some work. When it comes to illumination in the seat, aside from the overhead reading lights, airlines installed a smaller reading light. I like how the aircraft is fitted with air nozzles, giving us some freedom in adjusting the temperature in our seats.

Each Business Class seat is fitted with a large 18" HD personal TV. The picture quality is crisp and sharp, making watching shows on this TV pleasant. The IFE controllers Saudia uses resemble a smartphone, with most entertainment options controlled via touchscreen menus on the controller. BEYOND is Saudia's in-flight entertainment system, loaded with tons of entertainment options, from movies to TV shows to music. Saudia provides noise-cancelling headphones to all its Business Class passengers. Despite the ear cups' rectangular design, I still find these headphones comfortable to wear for a prolonged period, and they do a good job of blocking ambient noise. Despite offering complimentary Wi-Fi to Business Class passengers, I was not able to connect to the Wi-Fi on both legs of the flight on board Saudia B787 series aircraft.

Saudia offers a dine-on-demand concept, serving two meals for our 9-hour flight to and from Jeddah: a light meal service and a breakfast service. For the light meal, the three options that we had on the menu were Arabic Mezze, Chicken Satay and Lamb Kofta. I chose the chicken satay, which I found to be delicious. The chicken was tender, and the peanut satay sauce was flavourful. For breakfast, we could choose from the Saudia Sufrah set or from the à la carte menu. The à la carte menu breakfast came in two courses, and we were given three choices for both courses. For starters, we can choose between Greek Yoghurt, Fresh Fruit and Seared Halloumi; while the mains were a choice between Lamb Fettah, Organic eggs made onboard and Vanilla Pancakes. I opted for fresh fruit for starters and Lamb Fettah for mains. The fruits served were sweet and juicy, and the lamb was flavourful and well-spiced. There is a side dish of two pastry puffs that were stuffed with spinach. I am not a fan of these puffs due to personal preference.
translated_text:
Underrated Airlines with great Business Class experience The service onboard this leg was good, with a very attentive cabin crew. Although the service on board seemed to be taken from a page of the airline's SOP and executed by a very well-oiled crew, I could feel the attentiveness of the cabin crew working on my aisle. Throughout the flight, the cabin crew was kept busy with the different meal times. And when she walked by and saw that I was still awake, she offered to serve my light meal. After clearing my table, the same cabin crew came over to my seat with a tray of snacks, and at the same time, checked if I wanted any drinks to go along. During breakfast time, the cabin crew, seeing that I was awake, quickly heated up my breakfast order and served it after seeing I was settled down. The attentive cabin crew quickly cleared my table. We were presented with a retro white Saudia-branded amenity kit shortly after boarding. Inside the amenity kit, I found the Norwegian-branded Sprekenhus products such as lip balm, facial mist and hand cream, alongside eyemasks, dental kits and Saudia-branded socks.

Saudia houses all its 24 Business Class passengers in a single cabin onboard its B787-10 aircraft. Saudia B787 Business Class cabin looks clean and classy. The cream-coloured high-back seats bring out the black used in the framing of the IFE screen, while the dual-tone sand colour used in the inviting leather seat pays homage to the desert landscape in the kingdom, and the wood-grained finish on the seat's tables exudes a sense of luxury. The earth colour used in the carpet and the interior of the seat back throughout the cabin makes one feel relaxed. White walls and the bright lighting during boarding accentuate the cabin's clean look. There are three lavatories, one in the front of the aircraft and two at the back between the Business Class and Economy Class cabins. The seat felt comfortable even in its most uncomfortable upright position for take-off/landing, thanks to the thick padding and the soft leather used in these seats. In bed mode, the seat can recline to 180°, turning it into a comfortable flatbed on which we had a great sleep during the flight. Despite not having any doors in these seats, I still find a good amount of privacy in bed mode thanks to the high back of the seat. The lounge mode sits between the upright and flatbed modes, which are angled to provide passenger comfort. I like how the angles in these seats can be further adjusted, giving passengers the freedom to customise the seat angle to their preferences.

Saudia B787-10 aircraft Business Class is fitted with a large wooden table. The table, when fully deployed, is rather sizeable and sturdy, which is perfect for meals or for doing some work. I like how the aircraft is fitted with air nozzles, giving us some freedom in adjusting the temperature in our seats.

Rude on every level Terrible experience, not worth the money saved on flying other reputable airlines. Staff is almost universally extremely rude, as if it’s part of their training (or the lack there of).
Starting from check-in staff: very rude, not even a smile or a greet. Refused to ship zamzam water despite going for umrah. When I asked to speak to the manager, she waved her hand and said “he’s over there go talk to him”
Staff facilitating boarding wasn’t any more friendly. A passenger was verifying if he was at the right gate. The employee responded “can’t you read the monitor?”
Boarding was extremely hectic and unorganized. The announced boarding was complete as passengers were continuing to roll in and struggling to find overhead space. Take of was delayed 45 minutes and landing was delayed an hour and a half.
The flight attendants were very rigid, unpleasant. I don’t recall anyone smiling throughout the trip.
I think vigorous training is extremely necessary if they want to stay in business

raw_text:
Comfortable Seats with Narrow Footwell but good service Saudia only uses one door for all passengers during boarding, which marred the boarding experience due to the long wait in line at the door. Service started from the mandatory welcome at the door during boarding, where we were welcomed onboard and guided to our seats. Once we were at our seats, the cabin crew working on our aisle would approach each passenger to serve the welcome drink, followed by a serving of Arabic coffee. Saudia offered their recently refreshed amenity kit from ELIE SAAB, which contains a selection of skincare products such as face mist, hand and body lotion, as well as Eau de Toilette on top of the usual dental kits, eye masks and socks. The cabin crew came across as warm and kept her smile throughout the flight, despite seeing how busy she was. After serving the welcoming drink, the Filipino cabin crew later would return to take our orders for meals and the time we wanted to have them. She would subsequently reappear after her meal order rounds with all the passengers on her aisle, with the distribution of amenity kits to all passengers. Despite the busyness, the cabin crew was able to pay attention to detail and still care for all the passengers under her charge.

Business Class cabin on Saudia's A330-343 looked cramp, with all 30 seats packed into a single cabin. However, the cabin's colour scheme and the white lighting used during boarding gave it a clean look. The dual-tone sand-coloured seats complemented the cream coloured seat shells well. The drape of black colour used in the IFE screen and the side of the seat gave the cabin a touch of elegance amidst a mildly coloured cabin. The wooden grain centre consoles between the seats, and the earthy brown carpet, exude a premium vibe. There is only one lavatory serving all 30 Business Class passengers, located in front of the plane. The wooden grain used for the flooring and the dark brown used for the cabinet panels gave the lavatory a premium look. Saudia stocks its Business Class lavatories with additional in-house amenities, such as Eau de Parfum, hand lotion, and facial mist, in addition to the usual hand wash.

Saudia A330-343 Business Class seats are arranged in a 2-2-2 configuration, each comes with a seat pitch of 60" and a width of 19". One of the armrests can be lowered to increase the seat width slightly in bed mode. The thick cushioning and soft leather on the Saudia A300-343 Business Class seats are comfortable to sit on, even in upright mode. The comfort of these Business Class seats is very well translated to a comfortable bed when reclined 180° into a flatbed. I find a good amount of privacy in bed mode, thanks to the high-shell-back cocoon design of the seats. However, the narrow footwell in these Business Class seats might be restrictive for passengers when the seat is in bed mode. In addition to the flatbed and upright modes, the seat also offers a more relaxing lounge mode, a mode between the upright and bed modes. We found additional buttons that allowed us to customise the seat back recline angle, the calf rest angle, and the amount of lumbar support to our preferred angles that improved the comfort of the seat further.

Storage was the biggest weakness in the Saudia A330-343 Business Class seats. The only space we can store anything is the small cubby hole in the inside armrest of the centre console. This space is so small that I can only stow my mobile phone, a couple of charging cables and my passport. While we could use the seat pocket situated next to the headrest, the shallow, slim design does not seem to allow me to stow anything. Due to the footwell's small design, the space beneath is so small that I can hardly place my shoes under the footwell, let alone a backpack. There are two tables in each Business Class seat. The main seat table is stored in the centre console between the two seats. This table is sizeable and sturdy offerring ample space for one to dig into the airline's delicious meals or work on a laptop. I like how Saudia uses the space between the centre console as a side table. When it comes to powering up our lifestyle devices, Saudia fitted two USB-A charging outlets on the side of the seats on the black side extension (the headphone jack and IFE controller are also found on this black extension), on top of the international power outlets between the two seats. Each Business Class seat is also fitted with a small reading light next to the headrest of the seat.

Saudia fitted a 17" touchscreen personal TV in every Business Class seat onboard their A330-343. These large TV screens deliver sharp picture quality, making watching the airline's entertainment very pleasant. Saudia's IFE controller resembles a mobile phone, with minimal buttons and selections are made on the device's touchscreen. Each Business Class passenger is provided with a noise-cancelling headphone by Saudia. BEYOND, Saudia's in-flight entertainment system, is very well stocked with movies and shows, ranging from Hollywood blockbusters to regional movies and shows. Saudia also offers its Business Class passengers 10MB of complimentary Wi-Fi, which is only good for browsing and uploading to social media. However, connecting to the in-flight Wi-Fi requires us to enter a long string of ticket numbers, and it would not work again after one's phone went into sleep mode.

Saudia catered with two meals for this 6 hour flight, consisting the main breakfast and the light dining meal. For breakfast, the Saudia Sufrah option comprises Banana and Date Pudding with accompanying side dishes and traditional Arabic bread. For starters, in the à la carte menu, we could choose from Smoked Salmon, Chia Seeds Pudding or Fresh Fruit. The mains were choices between Spiced Salmon Frittata, Scrambled or Pan Fried Eggs, and Hong Kong Toast. And for Light Dining, it was a choice of Arabic Mezze, Bocconcini Salad and Wagyu Beef Burger. I opted for the Saudia Sufrah for breakfast. I had no idea what the pudding was made from, which tasted a little sticky. But the flavours from the caramelised banana and the toasted nuts from the Banana and Date Pudding were delicious. The accompanying manakeesh topped with thyme sauce was refreshing. I opted the Wagyu beef burger from the light dining. The beef was juicy, and the caramelised onion was tasty. The chunky chips were fried to perfection and not soggy.

raw_text:
Worst Airline customer service ⭐ 1/5 – Worst Airline Experience Ever

Flynas is honestly the worst airline I have ever dealt with.

I had a flight from Madinah (MED) to Dubai (DXB), and due to ongoing regional tensions, the flight was cancelled. The worst part is the way they handled it.

They sent me an email asking me to check in — even though the flight was already cancelled. This shows complete mismanagement and lack of coordination.

I tried calling their call center countless times, but no one answered. Instead, I kept getting recorded Eid Mubarak messages and was never able to speak to an actual person.

Then I tried contacting them on WhatsApp — it’s completely useless. Just automated replies, no real support at all.

Finally, when I managed to process cancellation, they offered me only 1500 SAR refund, which is unacceptable considering the situation.

There is literally no proper customer service, no accountability, and no real human support available.

Strongly advise everyone: stay away from Flynas.
Save your money, time, and peace of mind.

raw_text:
Disappointing and unprofessional rude staff premium Eco not wort it I recently travelled with Flynas under booking reference M8ESSF, having upgraded to Premium Economy with the expectation of a smoother and more efficient airport experience. Unfortunately, the reality was the complete opposite.
At the check-in counter, the staff member handling our passports abruptly chose to assist another passenger who arrived after us, leaving us waiting without explanation. This raised serious concerns about fairness and professionalism in customer handling.
Despite holding Premium Economy tickets, we were not initially acknowledged as premium passengers. I had to repeatedly insist before we were assisted at the correct counter, which completely defeats the purpose of paying for an upgraded service.
The baggage handling process was equally frustrating. Our entitled additional luggage allowance was not properly recognised, and our Zamzam water was refused for check-in due to our transit through Riyadh. However, I personally observed other passengers in similar situations being allowed to check theirs in. This inconsistency is unacceptable and suggests a lack of standardised procedures.
Most concerning was the staff member’s dismissive attitude and lack of basic customer service. The overall conduct was unprofessional and far below what one would expect from an airline offering premium services.
In summary, this experience reflects poor service standards, inconsistent policy enforcement, and a disappointing lack of customer care. I would strongly caution others against paying extra for Premium services with this airline until these issues are properly addressed.

raw_text: Abject failure in customer service. I wanted to book my daughter on a flight from London to Riyadh, she is 13, so would require a chaperone onto and off the planes. Plenty of airlines do this. The Website had no options, and made it clear I had to speak to someone. The phone number is entirely automated and did not have the right selections, and no option to speak to a human. I drove to King Khalid airport and the helpdesk said I had to go to the experience centre. I drove to the Experience centre (neither location is close to home) where they told me I had to speak to the airline desk in London. Riyadh is 3000 miles from London, and my daughter is nowhere near it either. This was a clear case of several people who could not be bothered to deal with a specific problem, which is actually pretty straight forward. Do not expect these people to do anything that isn't part of their daily routine. They will just refuse.

raw_text:
Threatened and Intimidated by Cabin Supervisor – Worst Airline Experience of My Life I am a British Pakistani citizen and a frequent traveller. What I experienced with Saudi Airlines was the worst airline experience of my life to my flight back from Karachi to London via Jeddah.

Shortly before take-off, the passenger seated next to me became severely unwell and was repeatedly vomiting. I politely informed the cabin crew and later calmly asked a supervisor whether another seat might be available. I fully understood the answer could be no.

Instead, the supervisor responded aggressively “Sit down on your seat or I kick you off from my flight, do you understand”. He then followed me to my seat, shouted loudly at me in front of other passengers, pointed his finger inches from my face, and repeatedly threatened to remove me from the aircraft, stating words to the effect of “This is my plane”. The behaviour lasted several minutes and was intimidating, threatening, and completely unprofessional. I remained quiet all this time when he was shouting on my face.

I genuinely feared for my safety. The incident was witnessed by many passengers, several of whom intervened and openly supported me. This incident was witnessed by many passengers, all of whom appeared shocked by his behaviour. When one passenger intervened and stated that his conduct was inappropriate, he responded by threatening to remove everyone from the plane if anyone complained or spoke up.
He then stopped the aircraft and attempted to have me removed from the flight.

Another crew member (Pakistani) approached me and asked me to apologise to the crew supervisor. I was deeply shocked by this request, as I had done nothing wrong. I refused.
I then decided to disembark voluntarily and took my hand luggage. At this point, several crew members, including two Pakistani staff, continued insisting that I apologise.

What happened next was extraordinary: numerous passengers who witnessed the incident stood up in my support, stating clearly that I should not be removed, nor asked to apologise. Several passengers stated they were prepared to leave the aircraft if I was forced off. Realising the seriousness of the situation, the crew supervisor changed his approach and allowed me to remain on board, and the aircraft departed.

After the flight, Saudi Airlines attempted to close my formal complaint with generic apology emails, provided no investigation findings, no accountability, and no compensation, despite the seriousness of the incident.

This experience left me humiliated, distressed, and psychologically shaken. It also raises serious concerns about passenger safety, staff conduct, and how complaints are handled. I am particularly troubled by whether such treatment is directed more commonly at Pakistani or South Asian passengers.

I will never fly with Saudi Airlines again and strongly advise others to think carefully before doing so.

raw_text:
Extremely Disrespectful Treatment of Umrah Passengers Our experience with Flynas was one of the worst travel experiences our family has ever faced.

My parents were traveling for Umrah from Lahore to Jeddah with a connection in King Khalid International Airport. Their flight from Riyadh to King Abdulaziz International Airport was scheduled for 10:10 AM.

This was their first international trip. They were already standing in the boarding line while other passengers were boarding. When their turn came, a staff member suddenly stopped them and told them the plane had already left.

The most shocking part was the behavior of the staff. Instead of helping two confused Umrah passengers, the staff spoke harshly and showed no respect or guidance.

Because of this situation, my parents were forced to purchase another ticket costing 400 Saudi Riyals just to continue their journey.

This happened during Ramadan while they were fasting. Even on the later flight they were not given even a simple bottle of water for sehri. That level of carelessness toward passengers, especially Umrah travelers, is extremely disappointing.

They were also left stressed and confused about their luggage because no proper information was given about where their bags were.

Air travel mistakes can happen, but rude treatment and lack of basic humanity should never happen. Passengers deserve respect, especially elderly travelers visiting for a religious journey.

I hope the airline seriously improves its staff behavior and passenger support.

Broken Verification System and No Real Customer Support One of the worst customer service experiences I’ve ever had.

Booking a ticket is easy—you can receive an OTP via SMS or email. However, the system becomes completely frustrating when you need to modify or cancel your booking.

In my case, I never received the OTP via SMS, so I used the email option to complete my purchase. But later, when I wanted to cancel the ticket, the system only allowed SMS verification—which I still could not receive. This made cancellation impossible.

I then tried their virtual agent and provided all required details, but again I was asked for the SMS OTP used during booking. The issue is that their system assumes I used SMS (because I tried it first), even though I actually completed the process via email. So again, no solution.

Calling customer service was equally disappointing. The representative said they had no authority to help and advised me to send an email, with a response time of 5–6 weeks—while my flight is in just 2 weeks.

This is an extremely frustrating and poorly designed system. It feels like everything works smoothly until they receive your money, but after that, you’re left without real support.

I strongly advise others to be cautious when booking, especially if flexibility or support might be needed later.

raw_text:
Terrible customer service from Saudia Dear Saudia,
I had a ticket booked for the SV1023 flight (with Saudia) from Riyadh to Jeddah on the 19th December, to fly at 09:00 am. I arrived at Riyadh airport by 7 a.m, checked in, and went through security. While in the departure area, I noticed the flight was not mentioned on any screen until around 08.30 when it was listed as delayed and continued to be re-listed as delayed at 11.00 am, 12:00 p.m, then 13:00 p.m. Eventually, a gate was provided but no staff to help explain the situation- a theme consistent throughout the day. Without even a hint of customer service, I continued to wait without any information. The few staff who did wander past were utterly unhelpful, offering merely vague responses. Meanwhile, other airlines, including Flynas and Flyadeal, operated normally, while Saudia's passengers were stranded without any accountability from the company.

After more than 12 hours, I received a message, again, no information from any staff (terrible customer service), that our flight was cancelled. Collecting my luggage took another hour of chaotic searching, again, with no Saudia staff present (was it a spontaneous holiday for them perhaps?)

After leaving the airport and going back to the check-in area, I finally found a customer service desk. There, a massive queue had built up, but I was told to leave as no one was going to receive ANY help unless they had a connecting journey. Apparently, the cause of the delay was said to be “natural causes", therefore making Saudia completely absolved of liability!

What makes this even worse is that Saudia’s own Customer Rights Policy states: 1 hour delay → refreshments. 2 hours → a meal. 6 hours → 150% ticket refund, rebooking, and hotel accommodation, etc. I did not receive any of this as the delay was apparently due to "natural causes". Instead, I was dismissed to “go home and wait.” This experience revealed that Saudia is an airline built on empty promises and a lack of accountability. This terrible display of poor customer service and hospitality is appalling.

On my recent flights between Dhaka, Jeddah, and Madinah, the cabin crew were inattentive, dismissive, and often more engaged in personal conversations than serving passengers. Requests for assistance were met with arrogance, and during a three‑hour delay, no proper information was provided. What’s most concerning is the disrespectful and discriminatory attitude shown toward Bangladeshi passengers, who were treated as though they had no right to basic courtesy. This level of service is unacceptable, and I strongly urge the airline to improve its standards and ensure fair treatment for all travelers.

This was one of the worst airline experiences of my life. My Flynas flight from Madinah was delayed by around 2 hours. We were clearly told that Sahoor would be provided, but that promise was completely broken. Instead of food, they gave passengers only water during Sahoor time in Ramadan. This was extremely disrespectful and unacceptable.

Because of this delay, I missed my connecting flight from Jeddah to Karachi. Flynas then placed us in a hotel, but the food quality was pathetic and far below basic standards.

After suffering a delay of nearly 24 hours, we finally boarded the next flight from Jeddah to Karachi. When we arrived, the situation became even worse: baggage was missing for around 30 passengers, including myself. Not a single bag arrived. No clear explanation, no proper assistance, and no accountability.

This airline showed:
• Zero care for passengers
• Poor communication
• Broken promises
• Bad food arrangements
• Total failure in baggage handling

I strongly do NOT recommend Flynas to anyone, especially for connecting or international flights. Save yourself the stress and choose another airline if you value your time, dignity, and belongings.

Terrible airlines My experience with Saudia Airlines has been extremely frustrating and unacceptable.

On December 19, 2025, I booked a flight from Riyadh to Dammam (Flight SV1132), which was cancelled by the airline. Despite repeated follow-ups over several months, I still have not received my refund, and no clear resolution has been provided.

I gave Saudia another chance and booked a second flight from Riyadh to Rome (Flight SV207 on March 25, 2026). Upon arrival in Rome, I was shocked to discover that none of the passengers received their luggage. The entire plane arrived without baggage.

It is extremely concerning that a flight could depart from Riyadh while leaving all passengers’ luggage behind. This reflects serious operational and logistical failures.

Now, three days later, my bag is still missing. The airline has been unresponsive and unhelpful, with no clear updates on the location of the luggage or when it will be delivered.

This experience shows a lack of accountability, poor customer service, and a failure to handle even the most basic airline responsibilities.

The Mumbai Jeddah flight was an older aircraft so the business cabin was 2-2-2 meaning if you were in the window seat, you had to jump over the person next to you. No WiFi and their approach to meal service sucked - I was in row 1 and they already ran out of my preference. That said it is a 5 hour flight and for that purpose it was fine.

The Jeddah JFk is a modern aircraft and the experience was generally good, at least for th price. Arrival in Jeddah had its own business class entrance and security line and was gas and seamless. There is a nice lounge as well although it was packed.

This flight has both first and business with first being private suites and business being 1-2-1 angled seats with full 180 lie flat. The cabin is modern, and they provided a pillow, blanket and toiletry bag. The menu offers lots of à la carte options and you can dine when you please. The flight was quiet and respectful and I slept most of it. The bathrooms seemed like they were cleaned throughout the flight.

My gripes:
WiFi coverage was not only slow and terrible, but the business code worked for a very short time and it was $500 for 200 MB otherwise. You can’t work on this flight. I tried to order two small items from the al a carte menu and i got pushback!! And of course, the flight is dry. Not a big deal to me but worth mentioning it.

In my case this flight was half the price of the others so it was good value, but I can’t see it’s competing with Quatar for example.

raw_text:
Bad experience I recently travelled from Jeddah to Lahore on Saudia Airlines (Flight SV732), and unfortunately, my experience was not satisfactory. The aircraft interior felt quite outdated, and the seating was very congested, making the journey uncomfortable, especially for a long-haul flight.

Additionally, the in-flight entertainment system was not functioning, as the TV screens were not working, which left passengers without any form of engagement during the trip. The quality of the food served on board was also below expectations.

I hope the airline considers upgrading its aircraft, improving seat comfort, and ensuring that entertainment systems are fully operational to provide a better travel experience for passengers in the future.
